Batch: 2022 – 2026 
 
Round 1: Online Assessment Test 
The test was conducted on an online platform and consisted of three coding questions that 
evaluated my problem-solving and technical skills. 
Question 1: 
Given a set of breadcrumbs for a website represented as an array of arrays of strings, 
construct a hierarchical tree structure from it. 
Sample Input: 
arr = [['Main', 'Dashboard', 'Settings'], ['Main', 'Profile', 
'Uploads'], ['Main', 'Dashboard', 'Products'], ['Main', 'Profile', 
'Remove'], ['Main', 'Manager', 'Work'], ['Main', 'Manager', 
'Review']] 
Expected Output: 
Main 
 -Dashboard 
  -Settings 
  -Products 
 -Profile 
  -Uploads 
  -Remove 
 -Manager 
  -Work 
  -Review 
 
Question 2: 
Solve the Josephus Problem. 
 
Question 3: 
You are provided with a group name and a list of people belonging to that group. 
Additionally, you are given an array of strings representing individuals standing in a line. The 
task is to reorder the array such that: 
1. If a person from a group is in the line, all other members of the same group should 
move to stand next to each other in the order they appear. 
2. Once all members of a group are adjacent, replace their names with the group name. 
I was able to solve Question 1 fully and Question 3 partially, which demonstrated my 
problem-solving abilities and technical knowledge, helping me progress to the next round. 
 
Interview Phase 
The interview process consisted of three rounds, two being technical and one HR. 
Round 1 (Technical – 30 mins): 
The interviewer began by asking me to introduce myself, including my interests, the projects 
I had listed on my resume, and how I approached learning new skills. He also inquired about 
my experience with platforms like LeetCode and other coding practice tools. 
After discussing these topics, he presented me with a logical reasoning problem: I had to 
identify the odd ball among eight balls, where seven balls were of the same weight, and one 
was either heavier or lighter. The challenge was to find the odd ball using a balance scale 
with just two weighings. 
Following this, I was asked to write pseudocode for a simple coding problem involving 
arrays. The task tested my ability to break down a problem logically and present a clear, 
step-by-step solution. The focus was on understanding my thought process and how I 
approached problem-solving in coding scenarios. 
 
Round 2 (Technical & Behavioral – 15 mins): 
This round was more relaxed and focused on my favorite and least favorite subjects. The 
interviewer asked technical questions related to the languages I was comfortable with, 
testing my in-depth knowledge. I was also asked about the time complexity of Quick Sort. 
 
Round 3 (HR – 10 mins): 
This round was a standard HR interview where I was asked about my background, goals, 
and why I wanted to join Appian. The interviewer inquired about my future plans, including 
whether I intended to pursue a Master's degree, and discussed my resume in detail. 
 
 
 
Tips: 
● Be Honest: If you don’t know the answer to a question, take a moment to think about 
it. If after 5 or 6 minutes you still can’t solve it, it’s better to be upfront about it. 
● Time Management: Don’t spend too much time on questions you are unsure of. 
Focus on answering what you know well. 
● Be Prepared: Have some questions ready for the interviewers, as this can leave a 
positive impression. 
● Research: Make sure to research the company before attending the interview. This 
helps in answering questions related to your interest in the company and its work 
culture.

BATCH: 2019-2013 
FOR: Internship 
NAME OF THE COMPANY:  VISA Inc, Bangalore 
ABOUT: 
Visa Inc. is an American multinational financial services corporation headquartered in Foster 
City, California, United States. It facilitates electronic funds transfers throughout the world, 
most commonly through Visa-branded credit cards, debit cards and prepaid cards. They are a 
global payments technology company working to enable consumers, businesses, banks, and 
governments to use digital currency. 
 
NUMBER OF ROUNDS:  2 
1. Online Coding Round 
2. Interview (Technical + HR) 
 
ONLINE ROUND: 
This round consists of two coding questions, given a duration of 60 minutes to solve it. 
General tip: Since there are no MCQs, try to solve both the questions completely and focus 
more on corner cases. 
The questions I was asked: 
1. An investor has some money and wants to ivest it in stock market. The number of stocks 
were given, and he can buy at most one share in any company. The total he invests 
should not exceed the funds available. A friend helps him make the maximum profit. 
Determine the maximum profit that can be earned. 
Given: savings, currentValue array, futureValue array 
Solution: same approach as 0-1 Knapsack. Here, n will be length of currentValue array, 
weights are the values of currentValue array, profits will be (futureValue – currentValue) 
and capacity is savings. 
Reference link: https://practice.geeksforgeeks.org/problems/0-1-knapsack-
problem0945/1 
 
2. A scenario of defect product were given, solved similar to the following problem: 
https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/maximum-size-sub-matrix-with-all-1s-in-a-binary-
matrix/ 
A total of 17 students were shortlisted. 
INTERVIEW: 
The interview was 75 minutes long. Started with “introduce yourself” following which he told 
me about him, his achievements and a brief about VISA.  Further I was asked about my projects 
and told to reason out my soft skills with examples. 
After bringing me to the environment, we further moved to the coding pair of the interview. 
We use hacker rank pair coding platform. I was asked to code two questions namely, reversing 
a linked list and implementation of a stack using two queues. Both the questions were told to 
be coded from scratch by defining structures and classes on our own and not the normal 
function definition we practice. 
After implementing the code and running it for the told test cases, I was told to include 
constructors, destructors, and inheritance in my second question i.e., implement a stack using 
queues, to produce a working code and again the code was tested against given test cases. 
With this the technical part was over and he further moved on to ask me DBMS questions. The 
questions I was asked include: 
1. Writing simple SQL queries 
2. Out of SQL, NoSQL, and Oracle databases, which is best and why 
3. For RDBMS, which database would fit best? 
4. Explain referential integrity 
5. Explain the concept of indexing and its advantages 
6. Difference between triggers and stored procedures 
After this he asked me about critical section problems and the difference between threads and 
processes. With this the subject related questions were over and moved to product-based 
question. 
The produce-based question alone went to a discussion of 40 mins where I was asked to build 
an application like redBus. I was first asked to draw a layout for the app considering it to be a 
huge app, following which he told me to explain my ideas on implementation and building the 
app. The interviewer told me to assume him to be the customer and hence correctly split the 
application into three modules and give him one module at a time, further discussing about his 
requirements and making necessary changes before giving him the next module.  
As the answer to the above I drew an ER diagram on the whiteboard and spoke about 
serverless architecture, AWS services such as lambda, S3 buckets and DynamoDB. I further 
included mailer functions, tokenization, and payments gateways. After presenting to him a 
consolidated layout I was asked to reduce the cost for which I removed transaction older than 5 
days, removed unnecessary payment gateways and reduced number of tables used. 
Lastly I was asked why I should be hired and with this the interview conclude. 
At the end you will be asked if you have any questions, please do not say no, instead ask a 
question related to the company’s achievement or new products. 
 
 
TOPICS TO BE FOCUSSED: 
• Data Structure 
• Database management system 
• Operating system 
• Whatever you’ve put in your resume 
• OOPS 
 
TAKE AWAY: 
Having a good knowledge in CS basics and coding along with creative approaches for product-
based questions will help in getting an internship in VISA. 
 
Wishing you all the very best!

took place at the first day and the other 3 rounds were the interview rounds that happened at the 
latter day. 
 
 
Round 1: Aptitude,Technical MCQ(30 Mins): 
 
Online test consisting of 30 MCQs to be solved within 30 mins on the Eduthrill platform. 
Questions were based on the basics of DS, sorting and searching, DBMS,Networks, Computer Ar-
chitecture, and C language. The questions were in the medium level of difficulty. I cleared the first 
round with the ease because I was very well prepared with the basics. 
 
 
Round 2: Coding Round (1 hr): 
 
 
The coding round happened in the Codelyser platform. It consists of two question 
where one was easy and another one was medium. 
1)The first question was that in an given array which has (0s and 1s) we have to find the number of 
1s which are present in between the zeroes. 
2)Rain Drop Problem.(Straightforward). 
 
In this platform ,the tricky part was we had  to enter the test cases to check our results and as 
well as we couldn’t see the test cases that has been passed which was used for evaluation. 
 
It was little bit different to accustom to the platform but I did solve 2 questions  in the matter 
of 20 mins. I did solve it fast because I knew 2 questions already. 
 
Round 3: Technical Interview Round-1: 
 
I got selected for  technical round which happened on the next day morning. The platform 
used for interview was Skype. The duration of the interview was around 1.5 hours. It was the best 
interview that I had ever attended. The interviewer was very much friendly and made me comforta-
ble so that I could answer him in the better manner. At first it was a basic introduction about myself 
and he even wished me that I had a good resume. After that he asked me to explain one of my pro-
jects which he founded to be interesting. 
 
After that he gave me an interesting question which I have not come across. I started to 
solve the question and it became more of a discussion rather than interview. I was asked to code the 
answer and it was screen shared to the interviewer. The way I used the data structure and coding 
style was very much standard. After so many discussion the question was solved by using BFS al-
gorithm. By listening to the question I couldn’t even guess it had to be solved using BFS. The inter-
viewer asked me to send the code to him for recruitment purpose. This was the only one question 
which was asked for the whole interview round. 
 
Round 4: Technical Interview Round-2: 
 
I got the call for the second interview round. It was started in the same manner as before 
round where the interviewer asked me the basic introduction. After that he asked me a basic DBMS 
question. After that he asked me a question in “Trees”. The question was to write a function for Post

certain test cases. After that he asked me to code the same above question in the iterative manner. I 
had solved that as well. 
 
 
 
Then the interviewer shouted me an another question to solve. This time it was with the giv-
en array and value ‘k’, I had to print the k-rotated array. I had solve it with brute force with 
O(n^2)and then he asked me to give the efficient solution. So I wrote the code with the efficient

my aspiration,I gave him some kind of cliche answer. He didn’t accept it so I gave a better one with

After that he asked me to code to find the angle between hour hand and second hand in the 
clock.It was quite tough and so I took my time  and gave him the answer. He accepted it and then 
move with another question which was something with time again. I gave him a solution but it was 
not a efficient one. 
 
Then finishing the coding part, he asked me “What is Denormalisation? Why it has to be 
done”. I told him  the answer which I thought it was right. After that interview is finished.

Citi - Intern Interview Experience 
 
About the company  
Citi is a global bank that offers a variety of financial services, 
including banking, credit cards, and investment solutions. It operates in 
more than 160 countries and values innovation, diversity, and teamwork, 
creating an inclusive workplace where employees can grow. Citi’s mission 
is to help people, businesses, and communities achieve their financial 
goals.  
 
Role: Application Developer 
 
Stipend: 75,000 INR per month  
 
Duration of internship: 8-10 weeks 
 
1.Online test (12.10.2025) 
​
The online test is conducted on the codility platform. It has 3 
questions. Among them 2 were easy and 1 was medium difficulty. 
​
 
1.​ Phonebook question - Easy  
●​ Input format 
​
​
{‘Ram’ : ‘123456’,​
​
​
 ‘Sam’ : ‘345689’, 
​
​
 ‘Raju’: ‘8976’ 
} 
●​ Search : ‘456’ 
●​ The task is to find the name whose phone number contains the 
given substring ‘456’. 
●​ If more than one phone number has the given substring we 
have to return the lexicographically smaller name. 
●​ In this case the phone numbers of ‘Ram’ and ‘Sam’ both have 
the given substring ‘456’. So we have to return the 
lexicographically smaller name - ‘Ram’. 
●​  If the input is ‘897’ then the answer will be ‘Raju’. 
●​ For this question they explicitly mentioned that there is no need 
to provide an optimal solution. Correctness is sufficient. 
 
 
 
 
2.​ Minimum number for given Digit Sum - Easy 
●​ Given integer X, find Y. 
●​ 'Y' is the minimum number whose sum of digits is equal to 'X' 
●​ Eg. If X=17, Y=89 
●​ If X=46, Y=199999 
●​ For this question they explicitly mentioned that there is no need 
to provide an optimal solution. Correctness is sufficient. 
 
3.​ Find the maximum round reached by each player - Medium 
●​ We will be given an array ‘skills’ which has the skill level of 
different players. 
●​ The adjacent players will compete in each round. 
●​ The player with the higher skill wins and moves on to the next 
round. 
●​ The process continues until only one winner remains. 
●​ We have to return the maximum round reached by a player. 
●​ Example 
skills = [2,7,6,3] 
Result : max_round =[1,2,2,1] 
●​  For this question they asked for an optimal solution . 
 
2. Technical Interview (30-35 mins) 
           The technical interview was conducted at CUIC on 05/11/2025. There 
were nearly 80 candidates got shortlisted for technical interviews. 
Everyone was randomly assigned to panels. I was called for my first round 
in the afternoon at 2pm. 
I was assigned to a panel of 2 interviewers. Both of them interacted 
with me throughout the interview. They first asked me to introduce myself 
and then they asked about my projects. I have done a realtime website for a 
startup. They were excited about it and asked several questions related to 
that project. 
Then they asked about my  Joint Secretary position at CSEA and 
Events Organizer position at CTF, since I mentioned them in my resume 
and self - Introduction. 
Then they asked about my DBMS project and asked whether I  was 
good at SQL. I said yes. So they asked me to write a Simple SQL Query. 
Then they asked some general questions. Then I was given the 
chance to ask questions to them. I asked about the company's tech stack 
 
 
and AI tools used. At the end they both wished me the best and the first 
round ended on a good note. 
 
Questions related to project  
●​ What are the key features of the project? 
●​ They also asked some questions about specific features. ( This part 
consumed  most of the time in the interview. They asked almost 
everything about the project)  
●​ Why didn't you suggest your client to use pre-established services 
like ‘Justdial’ for e-commerce. Why did you created a website for that 
when Justdial is there? 
●​ What are the challenges you faced while developing this project? 
 
Questions related to JS(CSEA) / Events(CTF) 
●​ What is your responsibility in this position? 
●​ Are you the only one in that role, or are there more people with you? 
●​ Tell about your experience in working as a team. 
●​ The same responsibility question is asked for CTF. 
●​ Asked some general questions about kurukshetra. 
 
Simple SQL Query  
●​ It is a simple query. Basically I was asked to join two tables and 
retrieve the relevant rows.  
 
General Questions 
●​ When you are submitting your code for review, your manager says 
‘Your code is bad’. What will you do now? 
●​ What was your favourite programming language? 
●​ Do you have an idea of going to higher studies? I said no. Then they 
asked why? I expressed my interest in developing real time 
applications. 
●​ Are you ready to relocate to Pune? 
 
3. HR Round (25-30 mins) 
     
I got called for the HR round within 7 minutes after I finished the 
technical round. In this panel there was only one interviewer. She first 
asked me to introduce myself and asked about my family members and my 
hometown. Then she asked about my previous internship experience. Then 
she asked about the flow of my project. I mentioned requirement gathering, 
 
 
Prototyping, Periodic reviews.  
Then she asked how did you gather 
requirements? What are the specific strategies you follow? Tell about any 
specific challenge you faced while developing the project? 
     Why are you interested in joining citi? 
     Do you have an interest in application development? I said yes. Then she 
asked why? 
    Then she asked whether I got any opportunity to work in environments 
that integrate computer networking and application development. I said no 
and explained how networking concepts like protocols helped me to 
quickly grasp the web server communication fundamentals. 
    Consider you are working in a team and someone says ‘no’ to your idea. 
How did you handle that situation? 
    Consider while working as a team you have to say ‘no’ to your teammate’s 
idea. How did you say that? 
     Then she asked Do I have any backlogs? Do any of my relatives work in 
citi? Am I ready to relocate to Pune? And am interested in pursuing higher 
studies?  
      At last, she wished me the best and suggested me to do research on 
networking + application development integrated systems and she told me 
that she worked on such systems and found them interesting. 
 
  After a few hours the results were released and 12 candidates got 
selected for the internship and I was one among them :) 
 
●​ In my experience, most of the questions were asked based on the 
resume  
●​ The interviewers were very friendly and the interview felt like a 
casual conversation. 
●​ The questions asked in the online round were also easy, but the 
submission time played a key role in shortlisting. 
●​ Stay calm and try to be yourself in HR rounds. 
●​ Never lie on your resume. 
●​ Never lose hope. Stay consistent. Keep putting the efforts, it will be 
always rewarded. All the best :)

VEGROW INTERVIEW EXPERIENCE  
(INTERNSHIP)  
Vegrow, India's leading B2B fruits marketplace was established in 2020 by IIT 
alumni. Vegrow has established a strong presence in the fruits segment in 
India, which is a large, unorganised market that is poised for tech-led 
disruption due to its fragmented and localised operational structure and is 
characterised by the involvement of multiple intermediaries facilitating 
transactions.  
The company maximises farmers’ income, by accurately grading produce and 
efficiently matching it with the most suitable demand channel. This creates a 
virtuous cycle, attracting more farmers through competitive pricing as well as 
more buyers, who seek consistency in both price and quality.  
ROUND 1:  
Online Hackerrank Test :  
It consists of three coding questions and an sql query to be written.  
0/1 Knapsack Problem  - The objective was to maximize the profit from 
selecting artifacts for a caravan with a strict weight limit. Given the profit 
values and weights of the artifacts, I needed to determine the maximum profit 
achievable without exceeding the weight capacity  
Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock - The task was to determine the maximum 
profit that can be achieved by buying a crop on one day and selling it on a later 
day, given an array of prices representing the crop prices over several days.  
Candy Distribution problem -  To calculate the minimum number of rewards 
(or candies) to distribute among fields based on their productivity ratings, 
ensuring that higher-rated fields receive more rewards than their adjacent 
lower-rated fields.  
SQL - SQL query problem where I needed to retrieve the names of students 
who scored higher than 75 marks. The results were ordered by the last three 
characters of their names, and if there were ties, they were further sorted by 
the student ID.  
  
From the test 10 students were shortlisted and eligible for the Interview 
Conducted at CUIC and there are 2 Technical interviews.  
  
ROUND 2:  
TECHNICAL INTERVIEW-1 : (1 hour)  
1. Introduce Yourself

SAP Labs – Internship 
 
About the company: 
SAP stands for Systems, Applications & Products in Data Processing. SAP is the largest non-American 
software company by revenue, the world's third-largest publicly traded software company by revenue, 
and the largest German company by market capitalization. SAP Labs India is SAP's largest Research and 
Development centre outside its headquarters in Germany and a part of a connected lab network of 20 
Labs in 17 countries. 
Originally known for leadership in enterprise resource planning (ERP) software, SAP has evolved to 
become a market leader in end-to-end enterprise application software, database, analytics, intelligent 
technologies, and experience management. It is a top cloud company with 200 million users worldwide. 
SAP Labs India drives SAP's product strategy and is responsible for SAP core solutions and provides 
product localization and India specific solutions. t is one of the earliest R&D centres of a global company. 
Coding round:

Duration: 45 minutes

Each of us got different questions. Both of my questions were based on string manipulation. I coded 
using C++. The first question was given a sentence which is the set of words searched in a dictionary I 
had to return the words that are to be stored in a dictionary which is basically the count of words 
greater than one. The second question was given a string I had to return the string after removing the 
vowels. 
Around 500 students attended this round out of which 15 students were shortlisted for the next round. 
Technical round 1:  
Duration: 1 hour 
Platform: MS Teams 
I was asked to introduce myself. Then I had to screen share and code on my notepad. The 
question was, given a linked list I had to reverse it and print. Then I had to explain my projects. 
After explaining I got a lot of questions regarding that. Then I was asked to explain about joins. I 
had to write a query for a given situation. Query was based on joins, order by and group by. 
Next, I was asked about OOPs. Then I was given a code and asked to predict the output and 
explain the code based on the OOPs concept. Lastly there were a few DSA questions. 
15 students attended this round and 8 were shortlisted for the next round. 
 
 
Technical round 2:  
Duration: 45 minutes 
Platform: MS Teams

question was given a month I had to return the season. I was asked to code the entire program. 
Then I had to explain my projects. I went for my DBMS project. Next, I was asked to explain the 
entire OOPs concept. And there were a few DSA questions about arrays, linked lists, trees and 
graphs. Then I got one debugging question. From Operating Systems Questions were from 
Paging, Segmentation, Fragmentation, Multithreading and Multiprocessing, Threads, Processes, 
Memory Management and Scheduling. Lastly, I was asked a puzzle. 
HR round:  
Duration: 15 min 
Platform: MS Teams 
This round was a casual conversation with the interviewer. I was asked to introduce myself and explain 
the projects. Then I had to narrate my experience with this virtual hiring process. Then I was asked 
which location I would prefer to work and why I would like to work for SAP Labs. Finally, they asked, if I 
had any questions for the interviewer which was asked in all the rounds. 
Out of 8 students, 6 were offered the internship. 
Note:

The first thing is to answer confidently. Even if you don’t know the exact answer take them 
through your thought process. 
• 
Don’t fake your resume and be clear about what you put in your resume.

I had to code the full program both in the coding round and in technical interviews. So, make 
sure you are prepared for that. 
• 
Cover all the concepts of DBMS, OOPs, OS and DSA. You can ace the technical interviews. 
• 
When it comes to HR round research about the company, never fail to do that. It would create a 
good impression on the interviewer if you know about the company.

text:
Arista Networks Interview Experience
Company Overview:
Arista Networks is a computer networking company specializing in software-driven
cloud networking for large-scale data centers, cloud computing, and campus en-
vironments. The company is known for its high-performance, scalable networks
that incorporate Ethernet speeds up to 800G, enabled by its Arista EOS (Extensi-
ble Operating System). Founded in 2008 by networking pioneers, Arista provides
solutions for artificial intelligence (AI), high-performance computing, and high-
frequency trading, focusing on availability, agility, automation, analytics, and
security.
Rounds:
1. Coding Round
2. Technical Round 1
3. Technical Round 2
4. Technical + HR Round
Coding Round
For the coding round only 80 students were shortlisted by the company. We did
our test in the computer system in our lab, not using our own laptop. It consists
of 3 coding questions along with 15 MCQs related to C, C++, and some DSA.
All three questions were given a time slot of 20, 25, and 30 min, respectively, and
for 15 MCQs in total, 15 min is given.
Qn1:
An array of size n will be given where adjacent elements are connected to each
other. Along with that, two points were given that imply an additional edge
connection between those points.
Now I need to return an array of length n
where the first index has the number of connections with a single hop, the second
with a double hop, and so on.
Qn2:
Two arrays, say arr1 and arr2, were given. We can assume that arr1[i] is the
parent of arr2[i] or vice versa. Create an undirected graph so that we can fulfill
1
our assumption. Now a 2D array was given called queries. Each query indicates
the ordered traversal of the graph. The traversal starts from 1, then visits all
children nodes, then their children, and so on. The output is a string where 1
says the query is correct; else, 0.
Qn3:
It’s a LeetCode problem that I mentioned below; the only difference is that they
asked for the largest value instead of the smaller one.
LeetCode 2462 - Total Cost to Hire K Workers
Focus on your problem-solving approach to provide an optimal solution.
Out of 80, 3 of us got selected for the next round.
Technical Round 1
It was an offline interview, and I was asked to code on the CoderPad platform
(pair programming interview).
Q1: LeetCode 71 - Simplify Path
Q2: Find the sum of the largest path in a binary tree; if we have more than one
largest path, return the maximum sum among those.
Then he started to ask about basic questions related to C, like about pointers,
the difference between char arr[] and char *arr[], and more, and also related to
Linux and OS, like ”What will happen if I type the ’ls’ command?” He expected
me to explain the internal working of the shell.
This round mainly focused on problem-solving skills as well as basic under-
standing of C, Linux, and OS.
Technical Round 2
This round was held online, and I started by introducing myself, and then he
straight away asked me to explain about the project that I consider the best.
He asked some cross questions about my project related to networks, and then,
similar to the first round, we discussed C programming in more detail. This time
we focused more on dynamic and static allocation and 1D and 2D arrays. Finally
he asked me if I got some questions.
This round mainly focused on in-depth understanding of my project.
2
Technical + HR Round
This round was also held online, and I was asked both technical and personal
questions. Technical questions were like some simple DSA coding questions and
also a scenario-based question on designing that involves giving an optimized
design to log data from some IoT devices. Finally he asked me if I got some
questions to answer.
Make sure your question is impressive.
Result
I am the only one selected for the position of Software Engineer FTE.
Key Takeaways
1. Try to provide optimal solutions.
2. You may start with a different approach but try to meet their expectation.
3. Focus more on DSA, OS, CN, Linux(shell), C, and C++.
4. Answer questions confidently.
5. Have a clear understanding of all concepts mentioned in your resume.
6. Maintain a CGPA of 8.5 or above.
7. Refer GFG to know more about this company.

APPIAN INTERVIEW EXPERIENCE 
ROLE: Software Engineer 
About Appian 
Appian Corporation is a leading American enterprise software company headquartered 
in McLean, Virginia. It specializes in providing a low-code automation platform that 
enables businesses to rapidly develop applications with minimal hand-coding. Appian’s 
platform combines process mining, workflow automation, case management, and 
artificial intelligence to streamline complex business operations across industries. 
The interview process consisted of 4 rounds: 
1.     Online Assessment 
2.     Technical Interview – 1 
3.     Technical Interview – 2 
4.     HR Interview 
Online Assessment: 
The Online Assessment had been conducted in Glider AI platform, which had a strict 
audio and video proctoring. It consisted of 3 coding questions focused on data 
structures and algorithms. All the candidates were asked same questions 
1. ​ First and Last occurrence of a given element in an array: Given a sorted array, 
determine the starting and ending position of a target value. 
2. ​ Insert new Interval: Merge a new interval into an existing list of sorted, 
non-overlapping intervals. The result should remain sorted and merged if overlaps. 
(Refer Insert New Interval in Leetcode) 
3. ​ Find duplicate and missing elements and return the status of the team: An 
array from 1 to n contains exactly 1 duplicate and missing number. A list of teams 
will be provided, each team consists of team IDs return which team consists of both 
the missing and duplicate number 
A total of 42 candidates were shortlisted after this round. 
Technical Round 1 
The interviewer began by asking me to introduce myself, followed by a brief introduction 
about himself. Then, we directly moved into a pair-coding session. This round was 
more focused on logical problem-solving than pure DSA. 
Problem 1 – Mobile Battery Drain Simulation 
I was given an array representing properties of multiples phones in a chunks of four 
elements each: 
·   ​ Index 0 -> Mobile ID 
·   ​ Index 1 -> Current Battery % 
·   ​ Index 2 -> Battery Drain rate per minute 
·   ​ Index 3 -> Relaxation time (in minutes) [Assume I use phone for 1 minute standardly 
and take break for phone[3] mins] 
This pattern continues for every phone. Determine which phone will get discharged first 
considering its use-and-relax cycle. 
Example: For [1,4,1,4,2,10,2,1] 
For 4% battery drains 1% every minute you use. After 1 minute of use, rest for 4 
minutes. So it would totally last for 16 minutes. For phone 2, 10% battery drains 2 per 
minute and is used continuously without break. So it would last for 5 minutes. 
I initially gave a wrong solution and simulated a few test cases. One of the cases failed, 
I asked for more time, rethought my approach, and derived a new solution which 
passed all the test-cases. 
For each phone, calculate the time at which phone hits 0%. Track the minimum and 
return the corresponding mobile ID. 
Problem 2 – Longest Word based on ‘BIG’ characters. 
Given a list of strings, find the one with the maximum number of characters from word 
“BIG” irrespective of the order. Example length of ‘bible’ is 2 (since ‘b’ and ‘I’ 2 
characters from “BIG” are present) and length of “bargain” is 3 (since all 3 characters 
from “BIG” are present). 
Project Discussion: I had listed AWS and a project involving AWS in my resume. The 
interviewer asked multiple questions on 
·       Specific AWS Services used 
·       Deployment Architecture 
·       AWS encryption techniques. 
We discussed on some-other remaining projects from the resume. 
Technical Round 2: 
This round felt more like a hybrid between technical and HR. The questions were 
focused on Achievements and Hackathons I participated in, What I learned from those 
events, The subject where I scored the lowest and why, and what I could have done to 
improve that performance.Also some questions on core CS DBMS were asked  
He asked me to explain one of my favourite projects. I explained its goals, tech stack, 
contributions and key takeaways. So the round ended with the interviewer asking 
whether I did have some questions for him. I asked some questions related to the SE 
role in Appian. 
After round 2, 24 of us got shortlisted. 
HR Round: 
The final round was straightforward and more focused on my academic record. 
Questions asked: 
1. ​ CGPA (mine is 8.68), 12th grade score 
2. ​ Whether I attended JEE or not (I didn’t) and the reason why 
3. ​ What LLMS I use and my knowledge on Java and spring boot and resources I used 
to learn them. 
And again the interview ended with me asking questions to the interviewer. 
Totally 12 students got selected as SE interns in Appian  
Key Takeaways: 
1. ​ The coding questions focused on logical thinking rather than algorithms. 
2. ​ Follow Striver’s A-Z Sheet and C.O.D.E intern materials for DSA preparation. 
3. ​ Be thorough with your resume and the projects you have included. 
4. ​ Learn about the Company before the interview and their primary tech-stack. 
5. ​ Before submitting the form, verify your details whether it’ s been entered correctly. 
All the best!! 
Feel free to reach out me: 
E-mail: ysripramod@gmail.com 
LinkedIn: linkedin.com/in/sripramod 
Phone: +91 9042389740 
Best Regards, 
Sripramod Yogarajan

Sriram B

AMERICAN EXPRESS 
ABOUT THE COMPANY: 
 
American Express (Amex) is a global financial services company, best 
known for its premium credit cards, payment solutions, and customer service. 
Founded in 1850, it operates in over 130 countries and is ranked among the 
world’s most valuable brands. 
For professionals, Amex offers strong career opportunities in areas such as 
technology, analytics, finance, operations, risk management, and customer 
service. The company emphasizes career growth, learning programs, and a 
culture of inclusion and collaboration. 
In India, American Express has a significant presence since 1921, with its largest 
center of excellence in Gurugram. Thousands of employees here contribute to 
global operations, innovation, and customer experience, making it an attractive 
workplace for those seeking growth in financial services and technology. 
 
JOB ROLE 
Intern/Apprentice – Technology – 8 weeks summer internship programme 
ELIGIBILITY:  
Minimum CGPA – 7, no active backlogs 
 
1. Online test 
 
The online test consisted of 3 coding questions and was conducted on 
codility platform. 
Questions: 
i) Given an array of integers. Find a subarray that starts and ends with the same 
number and has the maximum possible sum. Return that maximum sum. 
 
ii) given a row of houses, each containing bags of different types of trash: 
Metal (M), Plastic (P), and Glass (G). Distances between consecutive houses 
are given in array D, and the trash types at each house are given in array T. 
Each type of trash has a dedicated truck that: 
• Starts at the beginning, 
• Travels to the farthest house containing its trash type, 
• Collects bags (1 minute per bag), 
• Returns to the starting point. 
Task: Compute the total time needed until all trucks finish collecting and 
return, i.e., the maximum time among all trucks. 
 
iii) Given an n × m grid with an assassin A, guards (^ v < >), obstacles X, and 
empty cells (.) 
• Guards watch in a straight line in their facing direction until blocked by a 
wall/obstacle or another guard. All cells in their vision are unsafe. 
• The assassin starts at A and wants to reach the bottom-right cell (n-1, m-
1). 
• The assassin can move up/down/left/right on empty cells . only (not into 
obstacles, guards, or unsafe cells). 
• If the assassin starts in vision or the target is unsafe/blocked, they fail. 
Task: Determine if the assassin can safely reach the bottom-right cell. 
 
2.Interview 
 
Interview was held in virtual mode and consisted of two parts.  
i)Technical interview: 
Duration: 40 mins 
Started with self-introduction and projects that mentioned in my resume. I was 
asked to explain the features of my projects and how I have implemented 
them. Then, the interviewer asked me about the technologies I have 
knowledge and practical experience on. Then I was asked to code 2 questions 
in Notepad while sharing my screen 1. Using any programming language, 
merge all the files in a folder which couldn’t write the code completely but I 
explained the process and methods how it could be done  2. String reverse 
question with a little modification (reverse string word by word) which 
completely successfully. Then some questions on DBMS (on concurrency 
related to banking transactions and I was asked write 2 simple queries: finding 
duplicate entries but case in-sensitive and using group by and order by to find 
nth largest salary), Multithreading, OOPS (polymorphism and some other 
questions). Then the interviewer asked about my skills and knowledge on other 
tech such as AI, ML, Cloud etc,.. to which I replied that I have basic knowledge 
on AI did a course on it but haven’t done a project on. Then the interviewer 
finished by asking me do you have any questions.  
ii)HR interview: 
Duration: 55 mins 
Like the technical round, it also started with self-introduction and about my 
projects and the interviewer specifically asked many questions on how scalable 
is the project, the tech stack and the reason to use that and the team work and 
experience on working in the project and the interview slowly drifted towards 
my area of interests and expertise as I was asked questions like favourite 
subject and subject I hate and reason to choose CSE in CEG, the technology I 
am currently exploring and like to learn next, a technology that I don’t have in 
syllabus but I learnt it by myself and utilized it and liked it very much. Then 
some situation questions on a team project involving software development 
lifecycle questions and agile and other methods and my knowledge on that. I 
was also asked various other questions like how my team-mates describe me, 
my strengths and weaknesses, biggest achievement and failure, my inspiration, 
why Amex and why you are suitable for Amex etc,…                                    
My advise would be think before answering and answer the behavioural and 
situational questions via an example from your life experiences and elaborate 
on it and maintain a positive attitude even when you couldn’t answer 
completely and don’t feel worried about telling them that you haven’t learnt 
something yet. Be ambitious in your goals and sound confident. Try knowing 
about the company and role before the interview and it would be very useful.  
Wishing you all the best for your future goals. 
Sriram T 
Email: sriramthilagar@gmail.com 
Mobile: 9025724553

IDFC FIRST BANK INTERVIEW EXPERIENCE 
About the Company: 
IDFC FIRST Bank is a leading financial institution in India, formed by the merger of IDFC Bank and 
Capital First in 2018. The bank focuses on retail banking, corporate banking, and wealth management 
services. With an emphasis on customer-centric innovation and digital solutions, it has rapidly grown into 
one of the most trusted private sector banks in India. 
Role:Application Engineer 
CTC:18.1 
Selection Process: 
1. Online Test 
2. Technical Interview 1 
3. Technical Interview 2 
4. Managerial Round 
5.HR Round 
Online Test: 
• Aptitude MCQs: 10 (15 minutes) 
• Technical MCQs: 20 (30 minutes) 
• Coding questions: 2 (30 minutes) 
Coding Questions: 
1.Reverse a String 
   Input: "hello" 
   Output: "olleh" 
   I implemented a simple function using Python slicing (s[::-1]) to solve this efficiently. 
2.Diagonal Sum in a Spiral Matrix 
     Given a spiral matrix of size N x N (where N is odd), calculate the sum of the numbers on both diagonals. 
21 22 23 24 25 
20  7  8  9 10 
19  6  1  2 11 
18  5  4  3 12 
17 16 15 14 13 
Example: For N = 5, the spiral looks like above given one, 
The sum of diagonals is 101. 
Technical Interview 1 (45 mins): 
Introductory Question: Self-introduction. 
Sliding Window ( Banking Use Case): 
Question: The RBI continuously monitors bank accounts. If three consecutive transactions cross a 
total of ₹800, the account should be blocked for one day. Implement a solution. 
o I explained my sliding window approach: 
▪ Maintain a window of size 3 over the transaction array. 
▪ For each window, check if the sum exceeds ₹800. 
▪ If yes → mark account as blocked. 
o I wrote the code, explained it line by line, and discussed time complexity. 
Sample Input: [200, 350, 400, 100] 
Output: Blocked (because 200+350+400 = 950 > 800). 
SQL: 
• Query to print the second largest price from a table (without using LIMIT or subquery). 
• One question on basic GROUP BY usage. 
• Asked whether we can use GROUP BY without aggregate functions. 
• Asked one basic question to implement stored procedure in PLSQL(To print the second 
largest price from table after applying discount on it each row as unique discount). 
 JavaScript: 
• Difference between truthy and falsy values. 
Technical Interview 2 (1 Hour 30 Minutes): 
This round was a mix of OOPs concepts and in-depth project discussions. The panel expected clear 
understanding of fundamentals as well as hands-on knowledge of implementation. 
• 
OOPs Concepts (Java-focused): 
o Defined the four pillars of OOPs with two examples each: 
▪ 
Inheritance (single, multilevel, hierarchical, multiple – and discussed the diamond 
problem). 
▪ 
Polymorphism (static/compile-time via method overloading, dynamic/runtime via 
overriding). 
▪ 
Encapsulation (binding data with methods, explained with a class example). 
▪ 
Abstraction (using abstract classes and interfaces). 
o Explained the difference between interface and abstract class with practical use cases. 
• 
General Project Questions Across All Three Projects: 
o What was my role in each project? 
o What were the objectives of the projects? 
o What were the major challenges I faced and how I overcame them? 
• 
DBMS Project (Main Focus – ~40 minutes): 
o Spent significant time discussing the schema and ER diagram, explained attributes in detail. 
o Asked to draw the frontend of the project on paper. 
o Asked to write folder names and file names of the project. 
o Conceptual questions included: 
▪ 
Difference between frontend and backend. 
▪ 
What is DBMS vs. Database? 
▪ 
What is XAMPP and how was it integrated in the project? 
▪ 
How HTML forms work – mechanism behind the onclick submission button. 
• 
Machine Learning Project: 
o Asked to define ML to a person with no technical background. 
o Explained how to train and test a model. 
o Discussed the model I used, why I chose it, and differences compared to other models I 
experimented with. 
• 
Java Servlet Project: 
o Questions on What is Java Servlet? and its lifecycle. 
o Since I had used SMTP protocol in the project for sending emails, they asked about how 
SMTP works. 
Managerial Interview: 30 Minutes (Virtual) 
This round primarily evaluated my project execution skills, GitHub knowledge, and communication. 
• 
The manager asked me to open GitHub and explain one of my uploaded projects in detail. 
• 
He then went through all 6 projects I had uploaded on GitHub. 
• 
During the discussion, he tested whether I knew how to properly use GitHub commands and 
repository management. 
• 
I was asked to share my screen and run the project directly while explaining its workflow. 
• 
The manager also had a soft copy of my resume, and he referred to it during the discussion, asking 
clarifying questions on the listed skills and projects. 
• 
At the end of the round, he asked if I had any questions for him, giving me the chance to interact. 
HR Interview: 20–25 Minutes (Virtual) 
This round was conversational and focused on personality, mindset, and career preferences. 
• 
Began with a short introduction and family background. 
• 
Questions asked: 
o How was your overall experience in the previous rounds? 
o If not Engineering, what alternate career path would you choose and why? 
o Follow-up questions about that chosen career. 
• 
Scenario-based Question: 
o You are playing a cricket match. In one case, your team is winning but you did not perform 
well. In the second case, your team is losing but you performed really well. Which one would 
you choose, and why? 
General Tips: 
• 
Stay confident and calm – don’t put unnecessary pressure on yourself. 
• 
Communicate naturally – speak as if you are talking to a friend; this helps reduce nervousness. 
• 
Maintain composure – don’t be overly afraid; show a balanced level of confidence and positive 
attitude. 
• 
Build rapport – connect with the interviewers by greeting them warmly (e.g., a firm handshake in 
offline rounds). 
• 
Answer smartly – if you don’t know a question, try to relate it to relevant topics you are familiar 
with instead of staying silent. 
• 
First impression matters – give a clear, confident, and engaging self-introduction 
• 
Real life examples-provide real life examples whenever possible

NatWest Group Interview Experience (Internship) 
 
About NatWest Group 
National Westminster Bank commonly known as NatWest is a leading British banking 
and financial services company headquartered in Edinburgh, Scotland. Formed in 1968 through 
the merger of National Provincial Bank and Westminster Bank, it operates major subsidiaries 
including NatWest, RBS, Ulster Bank, Coutts, and NatWest Markets. As one of the UK’s “Big 
Four” banks, it serves millions of retails, commercial, and private customers, offering strong 
digital solutions while focusing on innovation, customer experience, and sustainability with a net 
zero commitment by 2050. 
 
Eligibility Criteria 
• 
CGPA: 6.0 and above from 10th onwards 
• 
No Current Arrear 
 
Selection Process 
1. 
Online Assessment (Offline @CUIC) 
2. 
Pre-Placement Talk (Offline @CUIC) 
3. 
Technical + HR Interview (Offline @CUIC) 
 
Online Assessment 
Online assessment was conducted on 25th August 2025 on the SHL Platform at CUIC. 
It consisted of four sections which could be attempted in any order. 
 
1. Coding (45 minutes, 2 questions, free navigation) 
• 
The questions were array-based and of easy difficulty level. 
• 
A driver code was provided; we only had to implement the required function. 
• 
Each participant was randomly assigned a different set of questions. 
 
Question 1: 
Given an array of integers and two values, start and end, return a new array containing only 
the elements that lie between start and end (inclusive), while preserving their original order. 
 
Input:  Array: [12, 76, 34, 98, 11, 54, 63, 45] 
Start: 30 
End: 60 
Output: 
Array: [34, 54, 45]  
 
Question 2: 
Given an n-digit number return the smallest possible n-digit number that can be formed using 
its digits. 
Input:  
Number: 90018 
Output:  
Number: 10089 
Although the questions were presented with additional filler context, the core expectation was 
exactly as written here. 
 
2. SQL (15 minutes, 10-12 questions, free navigation) 
• The questions involving selecting the correct option that produced the given output, 
determining the output of queries, and answering a few conceptual questions. 
• This section was relatively easy and could be solved with basic SQL knowledge. 
• It is recommended to be acquainted with SQL fundamentals and practice solving SQL 
queries beforehand. 
 
3. General Aptitude (36 minutes, 24 questions, no free navigation) 
• 
An optional practice test was available before the actual test. It is recommended to 
attend it, as the questions were quite different from those in other OAs. Attending this 
test also helps in becoming familiar with the platform. 
• 
The section was interactive, and the questions required scheduling meetings, adjusting 
bar graphs/pie charts to reflect correct percentage values (calculated from the given 
information), completing graphs based on node values, selecting dates, and assigning 
rooms based on given criteria. 
• 
Overall, the questions were of medium difficulty but time-consuming. 
 
4. 
English Aptitude (15 minutes, 12 questions, no free navigation) 
• 
This section comprised reading comprehension, synonyms and antonyms, error 
spotting, identifying the correct sequence of sentences. 
 
23 Students were shortlisted after this round. The shortlist was announced a day before the 
interview. 
 
Technical Interview + HR Interview (25 to 30 minutes) 
  
A PPT session was held on 29th August 2025 at CUIC. Generally, there was only one round 
of interview with two panel members. The interview for interns started at around 2.00 pm. The 
entire interview was based on my Resume and the answers I provided. 
Introduction 
I was asked to introduce myself. It is important to keep the introduction short and crisp, and 
be clear in advance about what you will share, as the panel may ask follow-up questions based 
on your introduction. 
 
Projects 
I was asked to explain all the projects I had listed in my resume. I described: 
• What the project was about, 
• Why I chose to do it, 
• The tech stack I used and the reasons for choosing it, and 
• The impact of the project. 
  
SQL query 
I was asked an SQL query related to one of my projects, It was an easy question involving 
JOINs and multiple WHERE clauses, they began with a basic query and then added a few 
follow-ups, and finally I was asked to explain my approach. I also provided an alternative 
solution. 
It is important to clearly explain your thought process and approach to the interviewers. 
 
Technical Section: 
• 
What is the difference between map and hashmap? 
• 
I was asked about my preferred programming language, the reason for choosing it and 
was also asked why I had learned four languages. 
• 
How strong am I in DSA? 
• 
What are the applications of linked list? 
• 
Since I had mentioned SDLC in my resume under coursework, they asked me to explain 
what I knew about SDLC. 
• 
I was then asked to explain about Normalization in DBMS. 
• 
They asked why I had used Google Colab (as mentioned in my resume). I explained that 
I had used it as part of my Gen AI coursework. 
 
HR Section: 
I was given a chance to ask questions—don’t miss this opportunity! 
 
I asked the panel about their journey at NatWest, and they were quite invested in 
sharing their experiences. I then asked about the involvement of AI at NatWest, their 
expectations from a fresher, and the kind of work I would be doing there. Finally, I 
asked for their suggestions on my resume. 
 
Finally, 9 students were shortlisted for the internship, and I am glad to be one among them.

My Personal Experience 
 
I started my preparation during the summer vacation of my second year. I used to solve 
2–3 DSA problems each day on LeetCode and GeeksforGeeks (though I had solved problems 
on LeetCode earlier, I wasn’t consistent back then). 
I didn’t really feel the heat of the competition until I started receiving notifications about 
internships and updates on selected candidates in WhatsApp groups. That’s when I sped up my 
preparation. 
For the OA, I brushed up on SQL concepts, revised general aptitude from IndiaBix, and 
practiced a few problems on LeetCode. I also went through placement experiences and solved 
problems similar to those. 
Note: Don’t forget to bring your calculator. 
For the interview, I would advise studying OS, DBMS, OOPS, and CN concepts in advance. 
Don’t postpone this until the last moment (which is what I did). Even though I wasn’t asked 
about OS, CN, and OOPS, many companies do ask them, so it’s better to be prepared. 
Note: Be thorough with your resume, I was asked about SDLC because I had listed it. Make 
sure you can explain your projects clearly: why you chose this project, its outcome, highlighted 
features, the tech stack you used, why you used it, and be ready for follow-up questions.  
 
General Advice 
•   Maintain proper posture during the interview. 
•   Communication is the key. 
•   Start your preparation as early as possible, but remember it’s never too late to start. 
•   Intern drives can be overwhelming but keep improving your skills and the results 
will follow.

Coding Question 1: 
Restaurant Queue Management: Calculate the total profit of a restaurant with limited seats, 
where guests arrive and leave according to a log, pay only once, wait in a FIFO queue if seats 
are full, and leave if they lose patience. 
Eg: 
n = 3 
price = [6, 2, 9, 5, 8]    logs = [0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 1, 0, 3, 2, 4] 
Explanation: 
• 
n = 3: 3 seats available. 
• 
price[i]: Guest 0 → ₹6, Guest 1logs sequence: 
• 
0 arrives → seat available → seated 
• 
1 arrives → seat available → seated 
• 
2 arrives → seat available → seated 
• 
3 arrives → all seats full → waits 
• 
4 arrives → all seats full → waits 
• 
1 leaves → seat free → 3 seated 
• 
0 leaves → seat free → 4 seated 
• 
3 leaves → seat free 
• 
2 leaves → seat free 
• 
4 leaves → seat free  
• 
Seated Guests: 0, 1, 2, 3, 4 → all paid once. 
 
• 
Profit: 
6 + 2 + 9 + 5 + 8 = 30 
 
Coding Question 2: 
The task is to write a program that determines and returns the current state of a tennis game 
based on the points scored by two players. 
 Tennis Scoring Rules Recap: 
• 
A tennis game is scored in points: 
o 1st point → 15 
o 2nd point → 30 
o 3rd point → 40 
• 
After both players reach at least 40 points, three special conditions apply: 
1. 
Deuce: 
▪ 
When both players have scored the same number of points (and at least 
3 points each). 
▪ 
Example:   5–5. 
2. 
Advantage: 
▪ 
When both have at least 3 points, but one player has 1 point more than 
the other. 
▪ 
Example: 6–5 (Advantage Player 1) or 5–6 (Advantage Player 2). 
 
3. 
Win: 
▪ 
A player wins if they have at least 4 points and at least 2 points more 
than the opponent. 
▪ 
Example: 8–4 → Player 1 wins. 
Example: 
nameP1 = "Alice" 
nameP2 = "Bob" 
winner = {"Alice", "Bob", "Alice", "Alice"} 
Alice scores: 3 points (15 → 30 → 40)  Bob scores: 1 point (15) 
 Output: "40 - 15" 
{If more points are played and it becomes 4–4: 
 Output: "Deuce" 
If it becomes 5–4: 
 Output: "Advantage Alice" 
If it becomes 6–4: 
 Output: "Alice wins"} 
➔Even though the tennis game rules look lengthy, the implementation logic is actually 
very simple. The whole problem boils down to a few clear if–else conditions based on the 
scores of the two players. 
 
TECHNICAL INTERVIEW ROUND  1 (45 mins): 
The technical interview was conducted virtually. Initially, the interviewer asked me to 
introduce myself, followed by a few questions about my projects — particularly focusing on 
the unique contributions I had made to them. After that, the session moved to a pair coding 
round. The problem given was the "Restrictive Candy Crush" problem GFG. The interviewer 
clearly explained the problem statement and asked me to first describe my approach with a 
dry run of the test cases. 
I explained the stack-based approach, where each number and its count are stored and 
updated as we process the input. She then asked me to write the pseudocode and explain it 
step-by-step, followed by an analysis of the time and space complexity. She also asked me 
to suggest the possible test cases that could be designed for this problem to ensure 
correctness and handle edge scenarios. This part of the interview focused more on how 
interactively I was solving the problem and thinking through different situations. The 
interviewer was satisfied with my approach and explanation. 
Next, the discussion shifted to concepts related to Locks and Semaphores. I was asked to 
explain read-write locks, two-phase locking (with an example), and provide an overview of 
locks, semaphores, and concurrency. I explained each concept with suitable examples, and 
the interviewer was satisfied. 
 
TECHNO MANAGERIAL INTERVIEW (R2): 
 
The  interview began with a self-introduction ,where I briefly talked about my background 
and interests. This  was followed by a discussion of my projects, where I explained  few 
projects and the reason for choosing the particular tech stack. The interviewer also asked for 
my opinion on Python versus Java and why I prefer one over the other,and asked me to list 
out the cases where Python can be preferred and then about which tech stacks I am familiar 
with and my level of experience with each and about the Software Development Life Cycle 
(SDLC) and its different phases in the design process. This was followed by, behavioral and 
Situational Questions: 
1.Conflict Management: “How would you manage a conflict between your teammates?” 
2. Tech Stack Issue Midway: “You realize halfway through the deadline that the chosen tech stack 
won’t work. What will you do?” 
3. New Technology with Limited Time: “If you are given a new tech stack you’re not familiar with 
and have only 2 days to complete the work while your teammates are busy, what will you do?” 
4. Team Preference:“Do you prefer working alone, in a team of two, or in a larger team?” 
 
The interviewer then returned to discussing my project work and asked how long it took me 
to adapt to a new skill, how I managed the learning process, and how I prioritized my tasks 
during the project. I utilized the last 10 minutes of the interview to ask the interviewer about 
Apple’s work culture and his journey of getting into Apple, which helped me gain valuable 
insights and left a positive impression. The interview lasted around 45 minutes. 
 
[The online assessment was conducted on 4th September, and the results were announced 
on the same day. My first interview round was held on 23rd September, followed by the 
second round on 24th September. This gave me around 20 days to prepare effectively.] 
 
 
 
 
KEY TAKEAWAYS: 
1.  Give a strong and impressive self-introduction – it’s the most important part of the 
interview as it reflects who you are and sets the tone for the rest of the discussion. 
2. Know your project work thoroughly – interviewers are highly experienced, so you 
must be confident and well-prepared to discuss your contributions, challenges faced, 
and solutions implemented. 
3. Practice coding consistently on platforms like LeetCode and GFG, and focus deeply 
on Operating Systems, DBMS, OOPs, Computer Networks, and System Design – not 
just theoretically but also with practical applications, as interviewers often expect 
real-world examples. 
 Pitch yourself effectively – most of the questions will be based on what you say, so 
be thoughtful and strategic while presenting your skills and experiences. 
4. Research the company thoroughly before the interview – understanding its culture, 
values, products, and work environment helps you align your answers better. 
 
                                                               ALL THE BEST!!! 
Keep practicing consistently — remember, every interview is a learning process that helps 
you grow and improve.

1. Verbal Aptitude 
2. Business Aptitude 
3. Coding Section 
This format is typical for Wells Fargo's online assessments. The verbal 
aptitude section tested vocabulary and comprehension. The business 
aptitude section focused on data interpretation from pie charts, bar charts, 
graphs, and stocks & exchanges. While these sections weren't particularly 
difficult, the time constraints made quick reading and analysis essential. 
The coding section featured three problems. The first two were quite 
easy, and most candidates could pass all test cases. The third problem was 
of medium difficulty, but the challenge was compounded by the use of an 
older C++ version, which didn’t support vectors. It was a graph-based 
problem with only two test cases. My code passed one of them, which 
turned out to be sufficient. 
Out of all candidates, 59 were selected for the next round, with 22 being 
added just an hour before the interviews through an additional shortlist. 
Tip:  
The aptitude section seemed to carry more weight than the coding 
part. I recommend starting to practice aptitude questions on platforms like 
IndiaBix and PrepInsta about a month before placements begin. For coding, 
LeetCode and GeeksforGeeks should be sufficient, but it's best to start 
learning DSA at least three months in advance, as online tests are becoming 
increasingly challenging. 
 
Round 2: Technical Interview 1 
In this round, the interviewer was friendly and helped me feel at ease, 
knowing it was my first interview. He started with a real-life problem that 
required sorting as a solution. I explained Merge Sort in detail, as it’s a good 
algorithm for in-depth discussion. He asked a few follow-up questions as 
well. 
We then discussed the differences between C++ and Java, and the 
interviewer dug deeper into this topic. Next, he moved on to system design 
and posed the problem statement: "Consider you're storing data about all 
Indians in a database. How would you design such a database? What factors 
would you consider?" I suggested a few solutions to improve access time, 
reduce latency, and enhance scalability. 
I felt I didn’t do well on the system design part, but the interviewer gave 
a positive response. 
 
Round 3: Technical Interview 2 
This round began with a basic question: "What is your favorite data 
structure?" I chose graphs and explained traversal algorithms. The 
interviewer asked several real-life questions, and I answered them using 
concepts like cycle detection, shortest path, and weighted graphs. 
The conversation then shifted to my projects. I walked him through the 
entire process, and he asked about alternatives to the stack I used and the 
innovations I implemented. We also covered some HR questions, like where 
I see myself in 3-5 years, handling conflicts within a team, and task 
assignments. 
Overall, this round went smoothly. The interviewer seemed pleased, 
and his feedback was positive. 
Tip:  
To succeed in technical interviews, it’s important to clearly explain 
your ideas and solutions. Don’t let anxiety or nerves get the best of you; 
mock interviews can help build confidence. Be thoroughly familiar with 
everything on your resume, as most questions will be asked from it. 
 
Round 4: Behavioral + HR Round 
This round was more like a conversation than a formal interview. I was 
expecting the usual HR questions, but the interviewer seemed genuinely 
interested in getting to know me. She asked about my passions and how my 
college experience has shaped my character. Since she was also involved 
in the pre-placement talk, I asked her a few questions based on that, and 
she seemed impressed with my attentiveness. 
The HR round lasted about 20 minutes and ended on a positive note. 
In total, 20 students were selected. 
 
 
 
Key topics to focus on include: 
o DSA (for Online Assessments and Interviews) 
o Aptitude 
o Core Subjects 
o Projects 
o System Design 
o Puzzles

Citi CSC Intern Interview Experience ‘23 
 
About the company: 
Citigroup Inc., commonly known as Citi, is indeed an American multinational investment bank and 
financial services corporation. Its headquarters are in New York City. Citi operates globally, offering a 
wide range of financial products and services to individuals, corporations, governments, and 
institutions. 
 
Citi also has a presence in India and operates under the name Citi India. In India, Citi has established 
Citi Solution Centres, which are specialized centres focused on technology, processing, analytics, and 
financial processes. These solution centres play a crucial role in supporting Citi's operations and 
providing various services to its customers. 
 
Citi's presence in India reflects its commitment to leveraging global talent and resources to deliver 
innovative solutions and efficient services to its clients worldwide. 
 
Number of rounds: 3 (After shortlisting of candidates above 8.5 CGPA by CUIC)  
1) Online Test  
2) Technical Interview 
3) HR Interview 
 
ONLINE ASSESSMENT: 
 
Platform: SHL AMCAT 
The test has few basic requirements: 
 
A Personal Computer / Laptop. 
 
A Webcam connected with the System (Not required in case of Laptop with functional in-
built Webcam) 
 
Smooth and uninterrupted Internet connectivity (throughout the testing session) 
 
Duration of Assessment: 2 Hrs 
 
The online assessment had 2 sections: 
 
APTITUDE SECTION 
 
CODING SECTION 
45 mins of Aptitude questions- 
In my experience, the aptitude questions in the assessment ranged from easy to medium difficulty. I 
found that calculating values and solving expressions took up a significant amount of time, making it 
a time-consuming process. What I noticed was that logical-based questions required careful 
attention because even though the question itself seemed easy; the answer options could be quite 
tricky. 
Do not spend too much time on a single question. It's crucial to manage time effectively and not get 
stuck on challenging questions. Instead, make it a point to move on to the next question (Note You 
can't come back to unmarked question again) 
 
What surprised me was that the aptitude round carried equal weightage as the coding round, 
highlighting its importance in the overall evaluation process. So, it's essential to prepare well for the 
aptitude section to maximize the chances of success. 
 
75 mins of coding questions- 
This section contained 2 questions. We could choose to code them in our preferred languages (Java / 
C++ is suggested) 
 
1. Program to Print the Trapezium Pattern: Given ‘num’ which indicates number of lines. The task is 
to print a trapezium pattern in num lines. (Program to Print the Trapezium Pattern - GeeksforGeeks) 
2. Couldn't recollect the question: something related to map.  
 
The questions were of easy difficulty, and I was able to pass all test cases in first question and 3-5 test 
cases in second question using brute force approach. 
 
TECHNICAL INTERVIEW: 
In my experience, this round was a face-to-face interview conducted physically at CUIC and lasted for 
30 minutes. From Anna University, a total of 48 students, including myself, were shortlisted for this 
round. We were divided into four groups based on our domains: FXLM, Risk, Support, and TTS. I was 
assigned to the FXLM domain interview. 
 
The interview commenced with the standard question of introducing myself. I understood that the 
answer I gave would create the first impression about me. Therefore, I decided to present my 
introduction in the form of a short story. I explained how I ended up pursuing computer science 
engineering at CEG after studying biology in school, why I chose this field, what I have learned from 
it, and shared my interests. 
 
During the interview, I was asked about how I managed to understand computer science concepts 
and subjects despite having no prior knowledge before joining college. I shared my experience of 
how my first year of college was quite challenging, and I had to find ways to grasp the concepts. I 
explained that I sought guidance from seniors and actively engaged in projects for each subject, 
which allowed me to gain a practical understanding of the topics. Implementing the concepts 
practically helped me comprehend them better. 
 
The interviewers also inquired about my projects, which I realized was an important question in the 
Citi intern interview. They were not solely interested in assessing my technical skills but also wanted 
to know about the innovation I brought to the projects and my knowledge of the languages, 
frameworks, and stack used. They emphasized how I collaborated with my teammates and 
showcased my leadership skills. I highlighted two high-quality projects that I could confidently 
present on my resume and to the interviewers. 
 
Additionally, I was asked about a few object-oriented programming concepts and database 
management system concepts, although I couldn't recall the exact questions. Lastly, they presented 
me with a puzzle to solve. The puzzle involved placing ten balls in five lines in a specific manner 
(referring to the link: https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/puzzle-10-balls-in-5-lines/). 
 
Towards the end of the interview, they gave me an opportunity to ask any questions I had, and then 
the interview concluded. 
 
HR ROUND: 
 
I am unable to explain about this round cause I had only 2 minutes of HR Round. The 
interviewer asked me to tell him about myself and asked me to leave : ). 
 
IMPORTANT TIPS: 
 
I have limited experience preparing for coding questions on coding platforms, and I consider 
myself fortunate to have received an easy question in my coding round. You can refer to the 
experiences of my other friends for more insights. 
 
 
When it comes to the aptitude round, it's important to practice time management by 
keeping track of the time taken to solve each question. Look for websites that offer aptitude 
questions and try to understand the underlying logic behind the solutions. 
 
 
Having a basic understanding of data structures, algorithms, computing complexities, and 
related concepts is beneficial. 
 
I recommend solving coding questions in Java or C++ rather than C or Python. It's often 
helpful to initially attempt a brute force method for coding questions, as it can satisfy most 
test cases. If you have no idea how to solve a problem, try hard coding specific solutions to 
satisfy the test cases. 
 
 
It's advantageous to have at least two solid projects that can enhance your resume. Ensure 
you have an in-depth understanding of your projects, including the problem statement and 
any novel aspects of your solutions. 
 
 
When preparing your resume, aim for a minimalistic approach. Include details that you are 
confident about and can answer during an interview. Order your projects and programming 
languages based on your level of confidence and knowledge. Avoid lying about any 
information on your resume and refrain from including things you are unsure about. 
Additionally, include any relevant extracurricular activities. 
(https://drive.google.com/file/d/1GxYBY-
Wbn63foJq2PDWRysVOUvxCQv8Q/view?usp=sharing) 
 
 
Before the interview, review last-minute preparation materials and frequently asked 
questions on object-oriented programming, database management systems, operating 
systems, data structures and algorithms, and popular puzzles. 
 
 
In Citi, the right attitude matters more than just technical skills. Be confident and 
demonstrate a willingness to learn and admit when you don't know something. Instead of 
simply saying "I don't know" to unknown questions in an interview, try to respond in a 
constructive manner. It's beneficial to have some knowledge about the company, the 
domain, and the role you are interviewing for at Citi. 
 
 
Lastly, it's worth noting that Citi is considered one of the easier companies to secure an 
internship with. They don't solely focus on technical skills. Stay confident and relaxed 
throughout the process. 
 
Don’t worry too much about the intern drive or give up in the middle. Sometimes luck plays a crucial 
role, and you will land up in a better place at right moment. All the best!!

Question 1 – Dataset Cleaning Cost You are given a string dataset of even length 
consisting of lowercase English letters. You can remove any two characters in one 
operation: 
If both are the same → cost = matchCost
If different → cost = mismatchCost 
Your goal is to remove all characters while minimizing the total cost.
Testcase 1:
dataset = "ouio", matchCost = 2, mismatchCost = 4
Here the matchCost is lower than mismatchcost so need to match as many chars as possible
'o' - 'o' got matched cost=2  'u','i' left in the string
'u' - 'i' got mistmatched so cost=cost+mismatchcost=2+4=6 string is now empty so return 6
Testcase 2:
s = "ouio", matchCost = 6, mismatchCost = 4 
Here the matchcost> mismatchCost so try to mismatch chars as possible to reduce the cost
'o'-'u' cost=4
'i'-'o' cost=cost+mistmatch=4+4=8 so return 8
Testcase 3:
s='ooouio' matchcost=6 mismatchcost=4
Here we have to match a 'o'-'o' no other way so ans would 14
From the question I figured out we are trying out different possiblities and choosing 
the minCost, So figured it as a Dp problem and coded a soln that passed 13/15 Testcases.

The 2nd question was a LinkedList question were I was asked Reorder the List.
I gave away 3 different approaches:
  1. Creating a new list with the same copy of value and reversing it, then 
  merging one from the given list and another from the reversed list.
class Solution {
public:
    void reorderList(ListNode* head) {  //I Coded the soln in paper
        ListNode* temp=head;
        ListNode* revList=new ListNode(0);
        ListNode* temp1=revList;
        int n=0;
        while(temp){
            n++;
            temp1->next=new ListNode(temp->val);
            temp1=temp1->next;
            temp=temp->next;
        }
        revList=reverse(revList->next);
        ListNode* ans=new ListNode(0);
        temp=head;temp1=ans;
        for(int i=0;i<n;i++){
            if(i>=ceil((double)n/2)){
                break;
            }
            temp1->next=temp;
            temp=temp->next;
            temp1=temp1->next;
            if((n & 1 && i!=n/2) || (n%2==0)){
            temp1->next=revList;
            revList=revList->next;
            temp1=temp1->next;
            }
            
        }
        temp1->next=NULL;
        head=ans->next;
    }
    ListNode* reverse(ListNode* p){
        ListNode *q=nullptr,*r;
        while(p){
            r=q;
            q=p;
            p=p->next;
            q->next=r;
Amazon SDE Intern(6M) Experience
4
        }
        return q;
    }
};

Tekion FTE Interview Experience
Intro:
-> Tekion is a software development company that develops business
applications for automobile industries and dealers.
-> For our batch(2019 - 2023) Placements started by 2nd - 3rd week of August.
-> Tekion came as the 15th company for our Placements and I had one Online
Round and 3 technical rounds and one HM discussion and finally HR round.
Online Round: - hackerearth platform
We had 5 coding questions.
1) Auto Suggest - (Edit Distance ,Levenshtein Distance) - 50 marks
2) URL checking - 20 marks
3) How many patterns - 20 marks
You are given a tree with N nodes. Only one of the nodes In the
tree is painted black and the rest are painted white. You can paint any white node as
black if it has an edge connecting a black-painted node.
Determine the count of distinct painted trees we can obtain by painting some of
the white nodes as black following the condition. Two trees are considered painted
differently if there is at least one node painted black in one tree and white in the other.
4) Triplets Count with modification - 50 marks
5) A Remainder Problem - 100 marks
You are given a large number N in the form of a string. An integer K
and Q queries are also given.
For each query, you are required to determine whether such
subsequences of N exist and following the provided condition:
When the number formed by concatenating the digits of N
without changing relative ordering is divided by K then it leaves the remainder X(each
number in the queries array).
If you can choose a subsequence then the answer for that
query(X) is “Yes” .
Else the answer is “No” for that query(X).
Example:
Input : N = 25 ; K = 5 ; Q = 2 ; queries = {0,1}
Output : [“yes”, “no”]
There is a subsequence “5” when divided by 5 gives remainder as 0(X).
So, “Yes” for the 1st query.
There is no any subsequence when divided by 5 gives remainder as 2(X).
So, “No” for the 2nd query.
For this bruteforce solution i.e generating all subsequences and checking
won’t work since the maximum length of the given string ‘N’ is very very large.

-> Then he asked what I did in my internship?
Technical Interview Round - 2:
20 students were shortlisted for the interview Round - 2.
-> This round was fully based on my projects.
-> I had put one DBMS and one ML and one Intern project in my resume.
-> I explained those 3 projects.
-> Then the interviewer asked what could I add more in the those projects and
how                             could it be implemented.
-> Then he asked many questions from backend and Database since he is from
the backend team.
-> How to overcome sql injection attacks?
-> How is the user authenticated and where are the authenticated details
stored?
-> How I stored the passwords in my Databases.
-> Then many follow up questions in backend and Databases.
Technical Interview Round - 3:(only few people had this round)
-> This round was very easy compared to the previous 2 rounds.
-> This round was a DSA + technical subjects discussion round.
-> He asked me to reverse a linked list using recursion and write pseudo code.
-> He asked what DFS and BFS were and asked me to perform inorder, preorder
and postorder traversals.
-> He asked me about the Round-Robin Scheduling algorithm.
-> He asked about different keys  present in the Database tables.
-> Then He asked whether I had some questions for him.
HM Discussion:
->This round was a discussion about what we will be doing in tekion.
-> Two people jointly attended this discussion.
-> If we had any doubts or questions about our role, we could ask them
to the interviewer( he was the head of the backend team).
-> This round was short and went for around 15 minutes.
HR Round:
-> The interviewer asked me to introduce myself.
-> Then she asked where my native place was located and then she
asked about my family background.
-> Then she asked about my strengths and weaknesses and why I call
them my strengths.
-> Then there was a one behaviour question like “You have an offer from 2
companies, one is from tekion and another from another company, Apart from money
what factors will you consider?”
-> Then she asked whether I had any questions for her.
Learnings and Takeaways:
-> Keep your expectations to zero.
-> Stick to your strengths and reduce your weaknesses.
-> Minimise your negatives and mistakes done in your previous
interviews and online rounds and try to solve those questions for which you did not find
the solution.
-> Have good knowledge in DSA, DBMS, OS,OOPS.
-> Before Any interview, revise all sorting algorithms(imp : merge sort ),
OOPS concepts, SQL queries, DBMS interview questions, OS interview questions.
-> Ask out for help from your seniors and friends when you get stuck( not
during the test :) ).
-> Interviewer will also help you when you get stuck.
-> Prepare answers for some standard HR questions and prepare some
questions to ask them to the interviewer.
Finally 12 people were placed in tekion including myself.
All the Very best!
Vijay Suriya S
Feel free to reach out to me at: linkedin.com/in/svijaysuriya/
