My SAP LABS Interview Experience: 
About SAP LABS: 
SAP (Systems, Applications, and Products in Data Processing) is a global leader 
in enterprise software, best known for its ERP solutions that help businesses 
integrate and manage core processes like finance, supply chain, HR, and 
customer service. Headquartered in Germany, SAP operates in over 180 
countries and enables organizations to run efficiently, sustainably, and 
intelligently 
Role:Developer Associate 
 
 
1st Round – Online Test 
The first round was an online coding assessment consisting of two questions: 
1. An array-based problem 
2. A tree-based problem 
Both were of easy to medium difficulty level. 
 
2nd Round – Technical Interview (Round 1) 
From the test, about 90 candidates were shortlisted for the interviews. 
My slot was late in the day, and I was called around 4:20 PM. The panel had 
two members. 
Flow of the Interview 
• Self-Introduction 
• Project Discussion 
o Explained my project. 
o Questions on which data structures I used and why. 
o Asked to normalize an ER diagram from my DBMS project. 
• DSA Questions: 
1. 
Merge point in a linked list 
2. 
Most frequent letter in a word 
• Puzzle: 8-ball puzzle 
• Club Activities: Questions about my involvement 
• SQL Queries: 
1. 
Using subquery 
2. 
Using joins 
• My Questions to Panel: 
o What are the skills an intern can learn at SAP? 
o How does a regular day look like at SAP? 
  Duration: ~50 minutes 
Takeaways: 
• Communicate with clarity and confidence. 
• Keep answers simple; don’t exaggerate (interviewers ask follow-ups 
based on your words). 
• Be thorough with your projects, as most questions revolve around them. 
 
3rd & 4th Rounds – Technical + Behavioral Combined 
Since my previous round ended late, my second technical and behavioral 
rounds were combined into one long session (~90 minutes). 
Panel 
• Senior Architect 
• Manager 
Flow of the Interview 
• Self-Introduction 
• Project Discussion 
o Explained workflow and security aspects. 
o Asked to draw and normalize an ER diagram for another project 
(not the one I had prepared). 
• Technical Questions: 
o API-related concepts 
o One SQL query 
o DoS attack 
o SQL Injection 
o OOPs concepts (4 pillars) 
o Java (multithreading, garbage collection) 
o Indexing 
o Deadlock 
• Behavioral Questions (by Manager): 
o “You say you’re a quick learner and adaptable—can you share a 
situation to prove this?” 
o “Tell me about your journey so far.” 
o Teamwork-related questions 
o Work-life balance questions 
Takeaways: 
• Technical: Strong grasp of core concepts (DBMS – especially 
normalization, CN, OOPs, SQL). 
• Behavioral: Be honest and always back answers with real-life examples. 
 
5th Round – HR Interview 
The final round was the HR round, which lasted about 20 minutes. 
Flow of the Interview 
• Self-introduction 
• Questions about extra-curricular activities 
• “Why SAP?” 
• “What is SAP?” 
• Discussion on package 
• “What will you do if we don’t select you?” 
• Since I mentioned reading books, he asked: “What was the last book you 
read?” 
The HR ended with a friendly note: “Let’s cross our fingers and hope for the 
best.” 
Takeaways: 
• Be genuine and positive in your responses. 
 
Final Result 
Out of 90 candidates, only 10 were selected—and I’m grateful to be one of 
them.   
 
Overall Key Learnings 
1. Confidence + Communication are as important as technical skills. 
2. Don’t overcomplicate or exaggerate—simple and clear answers work 
best. 
3. Be well-prepared with your projects and core subjects (DBMS, OOPs, 
SQL, CN, DSA). 
4. For behavioral rounds, answer honestly with examples from your 
experiences.

Tekion 
 
 
Tekion is a cloud-built platform focussed on transforming consumer experience in the 
automotive industry. The company uses advanced machine learning and AI capabilities for a 
personalised and seamless consumer experience. 
 
Selection Process consisted of: 
 
Round 1 - Online Test 
• MCQ section- Around 15 or 20 questions related to data structures, DBMS, loop outputs and 
other topics 
• Coding section consisted of one easy question, two medium and one hard. 
Below were my questions: 
➢ Easy- Given an array of nine stocks, return the largest number of stocks lesser than or 
equal to a number ‘n’. 
➢ Medium 1- Given an integer ‘n’ and two arrays, check how many elements of second 
array is present in first array. If number of common elements less than ‘n’, return -1, 
else return the maximum number of common elements. 
➢ Medium 2- Given two strings, how many characters needed to make a palindrome 
with the second string (substrings from the first string can be used to make 
palindrome)? 
➢ Hard- Given a number ‘n’ and two strings, if a value equal to or less than ‘n’ is added 
to each letter of the first string, is it possible to obtain the second string? 
I solved 7/10 test cases for the easy question and 2/10 test cases for the hard question. 
 
Round 2 - Technical Interview I 
• What is Polymorphism? 
• Difference between runtime polymorphism and compile time polymorphism. 
• Implement two stacks using a single array. 
• Implement three stacks using a single array. (I didn’t know the answer) 
• To code: 1. Implement stack 2. Given an integer n, find pairs of numbers in a sorted array 
equal to ‘n’ 
Language used:  C++. 
 
Round 3 - Technical Interview II 
• What is Paging? 
• What is Indexing? 
• What is Hashing? 
• What is an exception? What are the types of exceptions? 
• What is a Singleton Class? 
• To code: 1. Given linked list, point to nth last element. 2. Map data structure related question 
3. Singleton class 4. Exception example 
I coded the first two questions in C++. Interviewer asked if I was comfortable with Java. As 
I agreed, the interviewer asked me to code the next two questions using Java. 
 
Round 4: Managerial Interview 
This was an informal round and only consisted of questions related to my resume. 
The interviewer asked me about the projects mentioned in my resume, the programming languages I 
knew and about the certificates I obtained through online courses. 
  
 
Round 5: HR Interview 
This was also an informal round. Following questions were asked: 
• What are your strengths and weaknesses? 
• What was your favourite subject in school? 
• If not in this field, which profession would you enjoy to work in? 
• What are your hobbies? 
 
Note: All above interviews began with the question ‘Introduce yourself’. (Helpful tip: Prepare your 
introduction in advance. Try to mention points not already present in your resume like technical fields 
you are interested in and in case you have done an internship, what you learnt during that internship. 
Being well-prepared with your introduction will give you a huge confidence boost and will set a great 
tone for the rest of your interview.) 
 
Final Result: Selected :D 
 
Topics to be focussed: 
Data structures 
Object Oriented Programming 
Database Management Systems 
 
Java Basics 
 
What helped me through the placement process: 
• Keeping a positive attitude- Even if you don’t get shortlisted or don’t make it through the 
interviews, always remember these placement tests provide you invaluable experience in the 
form of type of questions asked in job interviews and knowledge of what companies are 
looking for. Know that the effort you are making is worth it and let this keep you motivated 
throughout the placement process.   
• Learning from mistakes- If there is a question you couldn’t answer in a test or interview, going 
back to it later and understanding where you went wrong ensures you don’t make the same 
mistake twice.  
 
Hope this helps  
 
 
All the Best!

AMAZON – INTERVIEW EXPERIENCE 
About the Company: 
Amazon is one of the world’s largest and most innovative multinational 
technology companies. With a strong customer-first approach, Amazon is 
renowned for revolutionizing online shopping through services like fast delivery, 
Amazon Prime, and Alexa-enabled smart devices. Its diverse business operations 
and commitment to innovation make it a dream workplace for professionals 
worldwide, offering unparalleled learning opportunities and a dynamic work 
environment. 
Role: Support Engineer 
Stipend : 50,000 + 400 USD per month 
During the month of November, Amazon conducted a hiring drive for the role of 
Support Engineer. It was the second time after August that Amazon conducted a 
drive, having previously recruited candidates for the role of Software 
Development Engineer. 
Overall Process: 
Online Test, Technical Interview I, Technical Interview II 
Online Test: 
69 of us who applied were allowed to sit for the online test comprising 14 
sections covering DBMS, OOPS, Programming, Networking, Error 
Handling, Software Engineering, Data Visualization, and more. It was all in 
MCQ format. We had one DSA question to solve (easy-level question). 
Technical Interview I: 
17 of us made it to Round 1, i.e., Technical Interview I. 
The virtual interview happened on the AWS Chime platform, and I had two 
interviewers. 
 Initially, they asked me to introduce myself. 
 In my resume, I had projects in Machine Learning, Full Stack 
Development, and Front-End Development. 
Question 2: You have done projects in several domains. How did you manage 
this? 
I answered: 
“Yes, sir, of course, we developed projects in several domains. The thing is, we 
would focus on a specific domain during each semester. For example, during 
our second semester, we focused on front-end development. In our fourth 
semester, we focused on back-end development and the database part. So, it was 
like a cumulative process, building on everything we learned. Therefore, it 
wasn’t that hard.” 
 Question 3: Explain your Machine Learning project. 
(Note: In all three interviews I attended, the interviewers never skipped 
asking about the ML project. Since AI/ML is everywhere, be thorough with 
your ML project if you include one in your resume.) 
I explained my project, Android Malware Detection System, and the 
algorithm I used. 
 Question 4: What was the reason behind choosing this specific project? 
What was the problem statement you had in mind? 
I answered with a real-life example I had faced. 
They tested my knowledge in DBMS asking 
How will you apply any condition in a query ? 
What keyword will be used to fetch unique values from a column ? 
Next, they moved on to DSA. 
A live coding link was shared where the interviewer and I could view and edit on 
the same platform. 
 
DSA Question 1: How will you find the occurrence of a character in a given 
string? 
https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/program-count-occurrence-given-character-
string/ 
I explained the approach using a hash map. They then asked me to code it (the 
logic function alone). Afterward, I dry-ran the code with an example and tested it 
against a test case. 
Even though the time complexity was O(N), the interviewer asked me to reduce 
it to O(N/2). At that moment, I came up with the idea of using a two-pointer 
approach along with a hash map. The interviewer was satisfied. 
 Scenario-based Question: 
What if I want the same alphabet in upper or lower case to be considered 
the same (e.g., ‘a’ and ‘A’)? How will you modify your code? 
I suggested converting all the characters in the string to a specific case 
(either upper or lower) before counting occurrences. 
DSA Question 2: Given an array, place the odd-indexed elements first and the 
even-indexed elements next. 
I gave an approach with O(N) complexity, typed the logic, and dry-ran it. 
Finally, they asked me if I had any questions for them. I asked for feedback on 
my performance, and they provided some. The two interviewers were very 
encouraging and appreciated every answer I gave. The process was smooth, and 
the interview lasted around an hour. 
Key Takeaways: 
 Practice DSA. 
 While coding, explain your approach. 
 Take care of edge cases. 
 
Technical Interview II: 
9 of us made it to Round II (Technical Interview II). 
Like Round 1, this interview happened on AWS Chime with a live coding link. 
Introduction: 
I introduced myself, and the interviewers introduced themselves. 
DSA Questions: 
1. Find the missing number. 
https://leetcode.com/problems/missing-number/ 
2. Loop in a linked list. 
https://leetcode.com/problems/linked-list-cycle/ 
I was asked to code and dry-run these with a test case. I solved and explained 
both questions. 
Linux Commands: 
1. How will you find a specific word in a given text file? 
2. What is the command used to edit a text file? 
3. One more similar question. 
I couldn’t answer all three correctly, but I attempted to think of and try 
something. 
OS Questions: 
1. What is a deadlock? What are the necessary conditions for a deadlock? 
2. Explain CPU scheduling algorithms with examples. 
I took four processes, assumed their arrival time and burst time, and 
explained how SJF (Shortest Job First), SRJF (Shortest Remaining Job 
First), RR (Round Robin), PS (Priority Scheduling), and FCFS (First Come 
First Serve) would work. 
Project Questions: 
They asked me to explain a project close to my heart, the tech stack used, and 
where JavaScript was used in my project and why. 
The interview lasted just half an hour and happened on November 14th. 
Key Takeaways: 
Be confident. 
It’s not about answering all the questions right. Give what you can. 
As I said , Virtual Interview happened on November 14th, 
All my friends departed for our national industrial visit (IV). I stayed back in 
college to attend the virtual interview, while a friend of mine attended an in-
person interview on campus the same day. After completing the interview, four 
of us somehow caught up with our friends in Bangalore and managed to join the 
IV. 
On the last day of the IV (November 18th), I got the result: 
Three of us, two from Computer Science and one from Information Technology, 
got selected. 
I was one of the two Computer Science candidates. 
 
 
A Personal Note: 
I am not a pro coder or anything, and I come from a bio-math background in 
HSE. I joined college with no prior knowledge of what Computer Science 
actually was. Of course, I felt inferior at times, thinking, “Coding isn’t meant for 
us.” But I realized that Computer Science isn’t just about typing and printing 
something on the screen. It’s about understanding the things that happen behind 
the scenes. It’s not merely about coding; it’s about understanding the concepts. 
Best of Luck! 
Resources I Followed: 
 DSA: Striver’s SDE Sheet, Nikhil Lohia’s and Striver’s YouTube videos 
(Personal suggestion : Nikhil Lohia’s explanations are top-notch). 
https://takeuforward.org/interviews/strivers-sde-sheet-top-coding-interview-
problems/ 
https://www.youtube.com/@nikoo28/featured 
 OS, DBMS, OOPS, C++: Career Ride YouTube channel. Their 20–25 
minute videos cover every important topic. You can still explore those 
topics in-depth. https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=careerride 
 CN, Linux: GFG, Interview Bit, and similar online platforms. 
Feel Free to reach out if you have any queries 
Email : tharaniarumugam514@gmail.com 
Phone: 6382033914 
LinkedIn : https://www.linkedin.com/in/tharani-
arumugam?utm_source=share&utm_campaign=share_via&utm_content=profile
&utm_medium=android_app

source_file: Tharun R S-Walmart Global Tech India.txt
text:
Walmart Global Tech Placement Interview Experience 
Role: SDE II 
Number of Rounds: 4 (1 online + 3 offline) 
Job Location: Chennai/Bengaluru 
Job Description: Walmart Global Tech is the technology wing of Walmart 
corporation. You will work on multiple domains like multichannel supply chain to 
complex retail systems in the point of sale, finance, logistics and transportation 
domains. You'll create game-changing solutions / applications using cutting-edge 
technology and streamline process through innovation. We're constantly reinventing 
how we save people's money in our retail stores, online and through their mobile 
devices, and we do it in an unexpected way. Whether you're analyzing data, 
simplifying the supply chain or creating solutions to make this planet more 
sustainable, what you'll be really doing is redefining the customer experience and 
making lives better. You get to work on a wide spectrum of technologies and 
heterogeneous platforms such as Java, Hadoop, Cassandra, ERP, Middleware tools, 
low latency messaging systems, machine learning and open-source frameworks 
 
Online Round:  
Platform: HirePro 
MCQs: 25 MCQs from OS, DBMS and OOPs 
Difficulty Level: Easy 
Coding Questions: 2 coding questions were asked. 
1. A string problem where given a sentence move vowels forward that are 
followed by a consonant. Don’t do anything for vowel followed by vowel, 
or consonant followed by consonant or consonant followed by vowel.  
And remove all spaces and special characters after moving of vowels. 
 
Sample input: "hello world! how are y#ou2" 
Sample output: hlleowrldohworaeyou2 
 
2. Given a list of integers nums, return a list of integers representing the no. 
of the factors of the each of the element in nums. 
Totally 14 candidates were selected for next round. 
 
Technical Round 1 (1hour): 
Difficulty Level: Medium 
I wasn’t asked any DSA questions in this round. The interviewer was very interested in 
my resume, and asked me questions about the skills I had mentioned in my resume. 
Since I had written Java as a skill, I was asked to write a simple API endpoint which 
fetches records from a DB. I was asked about my Internship experience, and what I 
had learnt. Java is not compulsory, but knowing it really helped me through this 
round, as I was asked lot of questions on Java. Answer honestly about your 
experiences as you will be tested on it.  
10 people were shortlisted from this round. 
 
Technical Round 2 (1hour): 
Difficult Level: Medium 
In this round I was asked to solve 3 DSA problems. 
1. Grouping Anagrams: https://leetcode.com/problems/group-
anagrams/description/ 
2. Given a big number, find the max product of k consecutive digits. Return the 
digits. 
Sample input: number = 567893 
 
 
 
K = 3 
Sample output: [7,8,9] 
3. Kth smallest element in a BST: https://leetcode.com/problems/kth-smallest-
element-in-a-bst/description/ 
 
Give the brute-force method first, then give optimized solution. It is done on pen and 
paper, so you just have to explain your solution as the interviewer wants to see how 
you solve problems. And be able to calculate time and space complexities. 
8 people were shortlisted after this round. 
 
HR Round (10-15 minutes): 
In this round I was asked about my projects, and why I chose to do these projects. 
Then I was asked what do I know about Walmart, and why do I want to work at 
Walmart. 
This round is your chance to show the interviewer that you are really passionate 
about technology and explain your projects and your achievements. 
Finally, 5 people were selected. 
 My advice: 
• Have a very good resume, use standard LaTex templates. Select your best 
projects. Include all the skills you have acquired. 
• Knowing Java is not compulsory but will be an advantage. I was asked lots of 
questions on Java, since I had mentioned it as my skill. 
• Be confident and comfortable in your answers. Don’t exaggerate your skills 
and be modest. 
• Showcase all your interests in technology. 
• Be good at DSA and calculating time and space complexities. Give all possible 
solutions you could think of during DSA round, even if its not optimized. You 
can discuss with interviewer about different approaches you could think of, 
and which you think is the best solution. 
Contact Info: 
Phone: 9030269853 
Email: rstharun11@gmail.com 
LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/tharun-r-s-3b8985229/

Citi Services India Pvt Ltd (Citi Bank) – Citicorp 
                                                    - Intern (2026 Batch) 
About the company: 
                               Citigroup Inc., commonly known as Citi, is indeed an American multinational 
investment bank and financial services corporation. Citibank, N.A. is the primary U.S. banking 
subsidiary of Citigroup, a financial services multinational corporation. Citibank was founded in 
1812 as City Bank of New York, and later became First National City Bank of New York. The bank 
has branches in 19 countries. Its headquarters are in New York City. Citi operates globally, 
offering a wide range of financial products and services to individuals, corporations, 
governments, and institutions. In India, Citi has established Citi Solution Centers, which are 
specialized centers focused on technology, processing, analytics, and financial processes. These 
solution centers play a crucial role in supporting Citi's operations and providing various services 
to its customers. 
Role that I chose: ICG (Institutional Client Groups) 
Another Role which was available: Personal Banking & Wealth 
Management Technology (PBWMT) 
Selection Process: 
The selection process consists of three rounds 
1. Online Round 
2. Technical Interview 
3. HR Round  
 
1) Online Round: 
                  
                        You should focus on mastering array-related questions, but learning other 
topics will also be beneficial. I was asked to answer DBMS query-related questions, and 
since I chose the ICG role, I encountered MCQs on React JS and Angular. The difficulty 
level of the questions was generally easy to medium. 
 
 
 
 
 
2) Technical Interview 
                           
                          “Don’t lie on your Resume!!!”. Most of the candidates who got into this 
round were questioned by only one panelist, and their questions were mostly based on 
the projects specified on their resume. If your provided with a problem don’t just provide 
them with your answer, drift your way through your entire thought process on solving it. 
Exceptionally on my case, I was questioned by two panel members and the questions 
were asked from all fields which includes  
 
• Project – Be thorough with your project 
• Data Structures (You can specify the data structure that you are 
confident with) – I chose array, stack, queue, BST, Heap. They asked 
me to reverse a queue using just the queue which can be done 
recursively. And some basic question followed by. 
• React – User Authentication 
• Computer Networks – How responses are handled in case of a query 
and what method will be used in each step, GET & POST 
• Operating System – How is a program compiled on accordance with 
OS, mutex, semaphores, etc…. 
• DBMS – Some queries. 
• OOP – Inheritance, generalization. 
• Recursion 
• Logical Question – There is a section in Geeks For Geeks where you 
can find similar questions. The question that was asked to me was 
“Rabbit can lie on Mon, Tue, Wed. Tortoise can lie on Thurs, Fri ,Sat. 
Today Rabbit says that it lied yesterday. Tortoise replied to that by 
saying ‘So am I!’ What is today?” 
• Basic problem-solving question. 
We were asked to do every solving in a paper, even the user 
authentication using REACT. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
3) HR Round:  
                            Gather all available information about the company and thoroughly 
understand the role. Review the job description shared on the CUIC portal. Ensure you're 
well-versed in the details provided during the Pre-Placement Talk. Be ready to answer 
common interview questions confidently, as they'll assess your preparation and planning 
for both short- and long-term career goals. Make sure to communicate your ideas clearly 
and confidently.

Email        : thyagesanprasath@gmail.com 
Linkedin   : Thyagesan Prasath.

Nokia-Interview 
 
Date : 21-08-2025(Thursday) 
Place : CUIC, CEG 
 
PPT – Congnizant Auditorium, CUIC 
Online Test – Rusa Lab CUIC – 20-08-2025(Wednesday) 
Round 1 – Technical Round 
Virtual Interview Connected to a Interviewer via Teams.  
How are you? 
Tell me about yourself.

Which language ? I know C,C++,Java. I am currently using C++ for DSA. So I am strong with C and C++.

Appian Interview Experience - Product Engineer Role 
Coding Round: The online coding round consisted of four problems aimed at testing data 
structures, algorithms, and problem-solving ability. Here is a brief overview of each question: 
1.​ Event Ticket Management System: Simulate a lottery-style queue reordering system 
using skip and offset logic, where IPL fans are rearranged in a virtual queue to ensure 
fairness before ticket sales begin. 
2.​ Passport Office Efficiency: Determine how many additional counters are needed to 
serve vulnerable applicants (children and senior citizens) at a passport office, based on 
the ages of the applicants and a fixed capacity per counter. 
3.​ Hotel Room Occupancy: Calculate the maximum number of hotel rooms that are 
occupied simultaneously, given a list of bookings with start and end dates. This helps in 
determining peak room demand. 
4.​ Store Inventory Search: Implement a search system to find how many times a query 
appears in each product name string and identify relevant inventory items based on a 
given query. 
After completing this round, I was shortlisted along with 42 other students for the interview 
rounds. 
Interview Round 1: 
The round began with a self-introduction, followed by a discussion around my previous 
internship experiences. I was asked about the technologies I worked with, specifically 
Kubernetes, and what I had learned during those internships.They then asked about the role of 
a Product Engineer at Appian and my understanding of the responsibilities it entails.The 
conversation moved to my personal projects, where I had to explain one of them in technical 
depth, along with the technologies used.Next, I was asked core DBMS questions related to 
indexing and normalization of a table.After that, a coding problem was presented where I had to 
determine if a student was eligible for a scholarship based on input details like grades and 
extra-curriculars. I implemented the logic using lists, for-loops, and if-elif-else statements.Two 
situational questions followed: 
●​ If a product you developed is running slow, how would you approach fixing it? 
●​ If you have an idea and the team is not supportive, how would you handle the situation?​
 
The round concluded with me asking a few questions about the company. 
Interview Round 2: 
This was a more casual interaction. The interviewer began by asking me to introduce myself 
again and then discussed my projects. He asked why I had applied for a Product Engineer role 
despite having significant experience in software engineering.The round ended with me asking 
questions about Appian’s work culture and environment. 
Interview Round 3: 
This was a short and crisp round. The interviewer mainly focused on my academic background 
and schooling. He again asked about my interest in the Product Engineer role and why I 
preferred it over a pure Software Engineer role. Overall, the process was a mix of technical and 
behavioral assessments, with a strong emphasis on problem-solving, project experience, and 
role fit. 
Tips :  
●​ Be clear on why you're applying for the Product Engineer role specifically. Have a 
well-thought-out explanation of how your background aligns with the role. 
●​ Make sure to revise key topics like DBMS,DSA, and OOPs, as they can form the basis of 
both technical and behavioral questions. 
●​ Be prepared to explain your projects in technical depth, especially the challenges faced 
and how you solved them. 
●​ Be honest and detailed about your previous internship experiences. Know the tools and 
technologies you used thoroughly. 
●​ Think out loud during situational questions. Structure your approach logically and show 
problem-solving skills rather than jumping straight to a solution. 
●​ Expect coding questions that involve lists, strings, loops, and conditionals. Practice 
problems that require processing structured inputs. 
●​ Always ask insightful questions at the end of the interview. It shows interest and helps 
you learn about the company’s values and work culture. 
All the very best!!

Appian Placement Interview Experience 
 
Pre-requisites: Python/Java/C++ 
Number of rounds: 4 ( 1 Online Assessment + 3 offline) 
 
Online Assessment 
3 
coding questions 
 
1.  Breadcrumbs in a website 
Given a list of list of string of paths to webpages in a website, generate the navigation tree and return 
the root node.  
Sample Input:  
[ 
[“Root”, “Profile”, “Settings”], 
[“Root”, “Profile”, “Albums”], 
[“Root”, “Settings”], 
] 
 
Sample Output: 
Root 
Profile 
Settings 
Albums 
               Settings 
 
 
Code: 
class TreeNode: 
    def __init__(self, name): 
        self.name = name 
        self.children = [] 
     
    def add_child(self, child_name): 
        for child in self.children: 
            if child.name == child_name: #i.e, child already exists 
                return child 
        #else, create a tree node and append to self.child 
        new_child = TreeNode(child_name) 
        self.children.append(new_child) 
        return new_child 
     
    def pretty_print(self,level=0): 
        print('\t'*level + self.name + '\n') 
        for child in self.children: 
            child.pretty_print(level+1) 
 
def construct_tree_from_breadcrumbs(breadcrumbs): 
    root = TreeNode("Home") 
 
    for breadcrumb in breadcrumbs: 
        current_node = root 
        for name in breadcrumb: 
            current_node = current_node.add_child(name) 
         
    root.children[0].pretty_print() 
 
2.  Given a line and a group registry which holds information on what group each person in line 
belongs to, re-organize the line such that  
a. if all the people of a particular group are present in line, then replace the first person of 
that group in line with their group name and remove the rest. 
b. order of people in line shouldn’t be changed for the rest 
 
Sample Input: 
line = [ 
    Person("Alice", "Group1"), Person("Bob", "Group1"), Person("Charlie", "Group2"), 
    Person("David", "Group2"), Person("Eve", "Group3"), Person("Frank", "Group3"),  
    Person("Eva","Group4"), 
] 
Sample Output: 
['Group1', 'Group2', 'Eve', 'Frank', 'Eva'] 
Code: 
class Person: 
    def __init__(self, name, group): 
        self.name = name 
        self.group = group 
     
def reorganize_line(line, group_registry): 
    group_dict = {group: [] for group in group_registry.keys()} 
    group_order = [] 
    person_order = [] 
 
    for person in line: 
        if person.group in group_dict: 
            if not group_dict[person.group]: 
                group_order.append(person.group) 
            group_dict[person.group].append(person.name) 
        else: 
            person_order.append(person.name) 
 
    final_list = [] 
    for group in group_dict: 
        if len(group_dict[group]) == len(group_registry[group]): 
            final_list.append(group) 
        else: 
            final_list.extend(person for person in group_dict[group]) 
    final_list.extend(person_order) 
 
    return final_list 
 
3.  There are n people waiting in line to buy tickets to a Taylor Swift concert. The ticket manager 
starts at an offset position m and distributes the first ticket. Then, the manager jumps k steps in a 
cyclic fashion to give the next ticket, and this process continues until all tickets are distributed. The 
task is to determine the order in which tickets are given out. 
This sum is derived from the josephus sum. 
Sample Input: 
n = 20 (total number of people) 
m = 14 (starting offset position) 
k = 4 (number of steps to jump) 
 
Sample Output: 
[14, 18, 3, 7, 11, 15, 4, 8, 12, 16, 19, 2, 6, 10, 13, 17, 1, 5, 9, 0] 
 
Code: 
def print_lot_order(num_of_lots, step, offset): 
    lottery_list = [] 
    for i in range(num_of_lots): 
        lottery_list.append(i) 
     
    #first draw 
    start_index = (offset + step)%num_of_lots 
    print(str(lottery_list.pop(start_index)),end=",") 
    num_of_lots -= 1 
 
    #subsequent draws 
    while(num_of_lots>0): 
        start_index = (start_index + step)%num_of_lots 
        print(str(lottery_list.pop(start_index)),end=",") 
        num_of_lots -= 1 
     
print_lot_order(20,4,14) 
 
16 were shortlisted for the interview. 
 
First Technical Round (40-45 minutes): 
1. Coding question – Given a set of numbers, print the Fibonacci numbers alone. 
 
Method 1: 
I gave brute force answer for this and optimized it as much as possible. I iterated through the 
list and found whether it’s a fibonacci number or not using an infinite loop, broke the loop 
when the Fibonacci number crossed the given number and returned true or false (i.e, 
whether a fibonacci number or not). Optimized by employing more breaking conditions in 
the initial iterative loop to reduce complexity and created an array to keep track of previously 
calculated Fibonacci values to reduce repetitive calculations. Was asked to calculate time 
complexity for this approach. 
 
 
Method 2: 
N is a Fibonacci number if and only if (5*N2 + 4) or (5*N2 – 4) is a perfect square. We can 
iterate through each element in the list and find out whether it satisfies the condition. Lower 
complexity than Method 1. 
 
Then resume related questions were asked. Questions about my projects were asked. 
Difficulties faced during projects. 
 
Second Technical Round (40-45 minutes): 
1. Coding Question – Given a string, determine whether one or more of its permutations would 
result in a palindrome. 
 
A stack can be used to solve this with ease. Push the characters in the stack and when you 
meet a character that’s already been pushed, pop it out. By the end of the loop, the length of 
stack must be 1 or 0. If so, then return true. 
 
Then Resume related questions were asked, this round focused on my intern experience. My 
intern work was discussed and questions related to it were asked. Like directives in angular 
(ngFor, ngIf, ngOptimizedImage etc. and their updated versions - @if, @for)

Key Takeaways: 
1. Connect with seniors who work there prior to the interviews (very important) 
2. Be strong in Java (you can give solutions in any language, but knowledge in java is expected)

thing about the company etc. Also communicate openly when you aren’t able to understand 
something.

very effective for them to understand your work and suggest improvements.  
8. Research on the company and know about their core values.

BARCLAYS 
This is an intern interview experience. 
About the company: 
Barclays is a leading British multinational bank with more than 325 years of history 
in financial services. Headquartered in London, it operates in over 40 countries and 
serves millions of customers worldwide. The company provides a wide range of 
services, including retail and commercial banking, investment banking, and wealth 
management. Known for its focus on innovation, technology, and sustainability, 
Barclays continues to play a key role in shaping the future of global banking and 
financial solutions. 
Selection process: 
There were 3 stages. 
Stage-1: Online assessment. 
Stage-2: Technical interview. 
Stage-3: HR interview. 
Online assessment: 
The first step in the selection process was an online assessment conducted on the 
HackerEarth platform in a virtual mode. Basically, OA had questions from OOPS, 
OS, debugging codes from various languages , predicting outputs for coding 
problems and 5 SQL queries. 
The assessment consisted of 25 questions to be completed in 70 minutes, 
including 20 multiple-choice questions and 5 SQL queries. To clear this OA, one 
needs a strong understanding of fundamental CS concepts. The SQL queries were 
super easy. But, it was given as a long passage and we need to figure out what they 
ask for. 
Though the questions were easy, TIME MANAGEMENT played a crucial role. So, 
manage your time accordingly.  
46 were shortlisted. 
Interview round: 
Interviews were conducted at CUIC. Before the interview session, we had Pre-
placement talk at 9.00 AM , where they actually made the interviewees comfortable 
and the recruiters were super-friendly. The interview session began at 10:30 AM. 
The interviewees were grouped into different teams and each assigned with a panel. 
Technical round: 
I had both the tech and HR rounds simultaneously, handled by a two-member panel, 
unlike others who had breaks. As expected, the first question was self-introduction. I 
briefly introduced myself and explained about my projects. 
After this, The following questions were asked. 
1. The tech I used in my DBMS project(Restaurant management system). E,g: 
Why PHP? Why no modern tech stacks? 
2. Asked about the project that I mentioned as currently-working. She verified 
whether I was really working on the project and asked about the different 
stages involved in doing a project. 
3. I was asked to write a SQL query : find the 2nd highest salary. Initially, I came 
up with the answer using subquery. Then, she asked me to optimise it and I 
managed to use OFFSET and LIMIT.  
• Which language I am familiar with? I said java. Series of questions were asked 
related to java -  
                   Collections 
                   Difference between Hashmap and Set 
                   Given a scenario, which data structure you would choose? 
 Why you use java? 
 Classes and objects in java.  
 
4. Explain about RESTful Api’s  
5. Explain different HTTP methods. 
 
6. Difference between PUT and PATCH. 
7. Explain your project workflow. 
8. Write a java code for the following scenario:  
a. Given a raw data file with various kind of information like name, age, 
phone number etc. Sort the file in ascending order of age and remove 
the rows where age is greater than 50. 
b. For this, I wrote the code using collections and using loops.  
 
    10.Explain about sorting algorithms. Specifically, she asked me to write the 
algorithm for bubble sort. 
11.Explain how REST Apis were used in your project. 
12.How will you handle a situation when 2 processes are simultaneously 
accessing the same resource? 
13. Some questions from DBMS and OOPS. 
That’s concluded technical part. 
 
HR round: 
A situation was described and they expected an answer that would be practically 
feasible. 
1. How will you work with senior team members? 
2. How will you react when your work is not recognized? 
3. She made reference of a Tamil movie Yaaradi nee mohini and asked whether 
a bug in real time project could be fixed in overnight. 
4. Will I accept the offer from Microsoft if given? 
5. What do you do other than coding? 
For every question, I aligned my answers with the values of Barclays, the RISES. 
It’s very important to know about the values and principles of the company you 
apply for. 
1. What RISES mean? 
2. Explain Stewardship in RISES with your real life example. 
3. Why Barclays? 
4. How do you see yourself in the next 5 years? (I answered that I aspire to 
continue working at Barclays and, like the interviewers, return to Anna 
University to recruit students from my own college).They found it unique 
and were impressed with my answer. 
Finally, they asked about my family, and my home. 
Out of 46, 15 were finally selected. I am happy to say that I am one among them. 
 KEY TAKEAWAYS: 
• Before attending internships, I covered 60% of DSA problems on A-Z Striver 
sheet. Consistent problem solving would definitely help in your process. 
• Be confident when you speak in interviews. 
• Always be truthful in interviews. If you’re not sure about a question, it’s better 
to say you’re not aware of it rather than guessing. 
• Be straight to the point. Don’t beat around the bush. 
• Know everything you mention in your resume. 
 
 
 
 
 
TIPS THAT WOULD HELP YOU: 
• Never underestimate yourself – you are capable of much more than you think. 
• If your friends get placed before you, don’t feel jealous. Everyone’s journey is 
different, and your time will surely come. 
• Give your best effort every time, and don’t stress over the results – success 
will follow when you stay consistent.

Gen Digital Inc. (formerly Symantec Corporation and NortonLifelock) is a multinational

Hiring Role:  Software Engineer Intern 
The hiring process had 4 rounds. 
• Online Test

• HR Round

Online Round: [30/09/2024] 
It consisted of three coding question of medium level difficulty 
Problem 1: Given a number n, convert it to its binary representation and store each binary 
digit in a linked list. The head of the linked list should contain the most significant bit 
(highest power of 2), and the head node cannot be 0. 
The task is to return the head of the linked list containing the binary form of n. 
This is a problem about converting a number to binary and storing the binary digits in a 
linked list format. 
Sample Input: 10 
Sample Output: 
1 
0 
1 
0 
Problem 2: You are given a string s of length n. Find the length of longest substring whose 
sum is divisible by 3 and if no such substring exists then return 0. 
Sample Input: 54641 
Sample Output: 4 
 
Problem 3: You are given a string s representing a number and an integer k. You can erase at 
most k digits from the number. Your task is to return the smallest possible number that can be 
obtained by removing up to k digits from s. 
This problem is about minimizing a number by strategically removing up to k digits from the 
original string. 
Sample Input: 4529350 
                       3 
Sample Output: 2350 
A total of 83 people were shortlisted for interview 
Technical Round 1: [21/10/2024] 
In the first round of the interview, I was asked to introduce myself, followed by questions on 
Data Structures and Algorithms (DSA), Database Management Systems (DBMS), and 
Object-Oriented Programming (OOPS). 
Coding Questions: 
 I was asked to write code for problems such as: 
• Checking if a given array can be formed into a Binary Search Tree (BST) 
• Reversing an array in place 
• Buy and sell stock problem 
• Maximum product subarray 
• Pairwise swap of elements in a linked list 
Data Structures and Concepts: 
The interviewer asked basic questions about linked lists, trees, graphs like differences 
between trees and graphs and types of trees, etc. 
Following this, I was asked 3-4 practical application questions. 
Example: 
A question, where a text message on WhatsApp can be edited and undone,what data structure 
can be used , I initially suggested using a stack. However, the interviewer pointed out that the 
data shouldn't be deleted as it can be redone. I then proposed using a linked list, where the 
edited message could be added as the next element, and when changes are undone, a pointer 
could move to the previous element, or to the next if redone. However, the interviewer asked 
to avoid pointers, so I came up with a solution using two stacks: one to store the current edits, 
and the other to store elements when they are undone, allowing for an undo-redo 
functionality without data deletion and she was satisfied with this 
DBMS: 
I was asked to write a query to find the second largest salary from a table. 
 
OOPS: 
The interviewer asked me to explain a concept in OOPS that made coding easier and how it 
differs from regular procedural code. 
After the interview, 35-40 candidates were shortlisted for the next round. 
Technical Round 2: 
This was also eliminatory round. 
In this round of the interview, I was asked questions on topics like Operating Systems (OS), 
Data Structures (DS), Object-Oriented Programming (OOPS), and projects mentioned in my 
resume. 
Data Structures (DS): 
The interviewer focused on testing my understanding of data structures, rather than 
definitions or explanations. 
I was also asked about adjacency lists and some tree-related questions. 
Object-Oriented Programming (OOPS): 
He asked me to apply OOP concepts to a building with multiple lifts and explain the 
communication between classes. Initially, I was thinking about how to apply inheritance and 
the four pillars of OOP between classes. However, the interviewer was looking for a basic 
answer—just the class names, which turned out to be "class Lift" and "class Floor." 
 (I 
was trying to overcomplicate it with code!). He laughed for overcomplicating. 
Operating Systems (OS): 
I was asked a question related to process scheduling. 
I was selected for the next round. 
HR Round: 
It was an eliminatory round, the interviewer asked me various questions related to my 
resume, including the tech stacks I used in my projects, club activities, and ongoing work in 
the clubs. I was also asked how many LeetCode questions I had solved, when I started using 
LeetCode, and what I am currently learning. I mentioned that I am learning Java, as I am 
primarily a C++ programmer, and many companies expect Java for cloud development.  
When asked about the next field I plan to learn (the interviewer preferred something other 
than cybersecurity since the company was Norton, which focuses on cybersecurity), I 
mentioned Artificial Intelligence (AI). I explained how AI can be both a cybersecurity threat 
(due to things like deepfakes) and a tool to protect digital users. 
Technical Questions: 
Difference between IPv4 and IPv6 
What is cache? 
Explain shared lock and exclusive lock in DBMS as if explaining to a kid. 
 
In the middle of the interview, the interviewer introduced himself and asked which team I 
would like to work with, then explained the different teams in the company. Before 
concluding, the interviewer advised me to specialize in a specific field, as many candidates 
had similar tech skills on their resumes, making it difficult to stand out. 
 
A total of 21 people were selected at the end. 
 
Tips: 
Be strong in DSA and core CS concepts like DBMS,OS,OOPS 
Practice leetcode regularly  
Be bold and confident while answering 
Ask for hints if you aren’t able to think of a solution rather than saying I don’t know. 
All The Best!!!

TEKION INTERVIEW EXPERIENCE 
About the Company: 
Tekion, founded in 2016 by former Tesla CIO Jay Vijayan, is a fast-growing cloud 
technology company revolutionizing the automotive retail industry. Headquartered in Pleasanton, 
California with major tech hubs in India, Tekion serves over 2,000 dealerships and 52 OEM brands 
worldwide through its flagship Automotive Retail Cloud (ARC) platform. 
 
Number of Rounds: 4 
1. Online Assessment 
2. Technical Interview - 1 
3. Technical Interview - 2 
4. HR Interview 
 
Online Assessment: 
MCQ - 20 Questions (DBMS, OS, OOPS, DSA) 
DSA: 
Problem 1: 
You are given two arrays, marie and annie, which represent the face cards of two players, and a 
string flip that contains either "Even" or "Odd". The game is played in rounds, where each index i 
of the arrays represents one round. 
• 
If flip = "Even", then the players calculate their scores as: 
o Marie’s score for this round = abs(marie[i] – annie[i]) 
o Annie’s score for this round = abs(annie[i] – marie[i]) 
• 
If flip = "Odd", both players skip their cards for that round (no score is added). 
After each round, the value of flip toggles (i.e., "Even" becomes "Odd", and "Odd" becomes 
"Even"). 
Your task is to find the final score of Marie and Annie after all rounds. 
 
Example: 
Input: 
marie = [4, 5, 6, 7]   
annie = [1, 2, 3, 4]   
flip = "Even" 
Process: 
• 
Round 1 (flip = "Even"): 
Marie = |4 – 1| = 3 
Annie = |1 – 4| = 3 
• 
Round 2 (flip = "Odd"): skipped 
• 
Round 3 (flip = "Even"): 
Marie = |6 – 3| = 3 
Annie = |3 – 6| = 3 
• 
Round 4 (flip = "Odd"): skipped 
Final Scores: 
Marie = 3 + 3 = 6   
Annie = 3 + 3 = 6 
 
Problem 2: Bandwidth Allocation (0/1 Knapsack Variant) 
You are given: 
• 
An array bandwidthNeeded[ ], where bandwidthNeeded[i] represents the amount of 
bandwidth required to process the i-th process. 
• 
An array requestsProcessed[ ], where requestsProcessed[i] represents the number of 
requests that can be processed if the i-th process is allocated its required bandwidth. 
• 
An integer totalBandwidth, which represents the maximum total bandwidth available. 
You need to allocate the available bandwidth to processes in such a way that: 
• 
A process is either fully allocated its required bandwidth or not allocated at all (no partial 
allocation). 
• 
The total allocated bandwidth does not exceed totalBandwidth. 
• 
The total number of requests processed is maximized. 
Your task is to determine the maximum number of requests that can be processed with the given 
total bandwidth. 
Example 
Input: 
bandwidthNeeded = [2, 3, 4, 5]   
requestsProcessed = [3, 4, 5, 6]   
totalBandwidth = 5 
Explanation: 
• 
If we choose processes 1 and 2: bandwidth used = 2 + 3 = 5 → requests processed = 3 + 4 
= 7 
• 
If we choose processes 3: bandwidth used = 4 → requests processed = 5 
• 
If we choose process 4: bandwidth used = 5 → requests processed = 6 
The optimal allocation is processes 1 and 2 → 7 requests. 
Output: 
Maximum requests processed = 7 
We took the online assessment on the HackerRank platform at CUIC. I managed to solve the first 
question completely and the second question partially, and I did well in the MCQ section. A total of 
30 students from CSE were shortlisted for the next round. 
 
Technical Interview 1 (50 minutes) 
This round mainly focused on DSA and Core subjects. 
• 
The interviewer first went through my resume and asked about my projects and the tech 
stacks I had worked with. 
• 
Since I had mentioned NSS under campus involvements, he asked about that as well. 
• 
He then asked me about my favorite subject. I answered DBMS, so he followed up with 
several DBMS-related questions. 
 
DBMS Questions 
1. What is replication, sharding, and partitioning? 
2. What is the CAP theorem? 
3. What is a transaction? 
4. What are the ACID properties? 
5. What are the different types of joins? 
6. What is functional dependency? 
7. What is a read/write conflict? 
8. What is copy-on-write? 
DSA Problems 
He then moved on to DSA and asked me three problems: 
1. Given an array and integer k, left shift the array by k times. 
2. Find the parent node of a given node in a binary tree. 
3. Find the intersection node of two linked lists. 
I provided both brute-force and optimal approaches for all the problems and wrote the pseudo-
code as requested. The interviewer was satisfied with my solutions. 
In the middle of the interview, I was also asked to introduce myself. 
OS Questions 
Later, the interviewer asked if I was familiar with OS concepts. Since I said yes, he asked the 
following questions: 
1. What are semaphores? 
2. What is a mutex? 
3. What is a process? 
4. What is a thread? 
5. What are paging and segmentation? 
6. What is a page fault? 
7. What is thrashing? 
8. What is a deadlock? 
Finally, the interviewer asked whether I had any questions. I asked two questions in return. 
 
Technical Interview 2 (45 minutes) 
For the first 10 minutes, the interviewer introduced himself and spoke about the company. After 
that, I was asked to introduce myself. 
This round was mainly focused on projects. 
• 
He asked about my favorite project, and I explained my CIP project. 
• 
He asked several follow-up questions, such as: 
o What tech stack did I use? 
o Why did I choose that particular stack? 
o Have I worked with AWS, and what services do I know? 
• 
He also gave me a few scenarios and asked how my application would behave in those 
cases. 
• 
I was then asked to design an algorithm for a scenario related to my project and explain its 
time complexity. 
The interviewer also asked a DSA problem: 
Problem: 
Given an array of strings, remove all the strings that have a permutation of themselves present in 
the array. 
I provided both a brute-force and an optimal approach and discussed the time complexity for each. 
Towards the end, he asked a few questions from Operating Systems (OS) as well. 
Finally, the interviewer asked whether I had any questions, and I asked a couple of questions in 
return. 
 
HR Interview (15 minutes) 
This round was more of a casual conversation. 
• 
The interviewer first introduced herself. 
• 
She then asked me to introduce myself. 
• 
I was asked whether I had attended the pre-placement talk. 
• 
She asked what I think about the company and what makes me a good fit for the role. 
• 
I was also asked about the key takeaways from my projects and to explain all of them 
briefly. 
• 
She asked me to share any conflicts I had faced with my teammates and how I managed 
them. 
• 
Finally, she asked whether I had any questions, and I asked a couple in return. 
At the end of the process, 9 students from CSE were selected, and I was one of them. 
 
Key Notes: 
• 
Revise your core subjects like DBMS, OS, Computer Networks, and DSA thoroughly before 
the interview. 
• 
Practice solving coding problems and be prepared to explain both brute force and optimal 
approaches. 
• 
Always mention the time and space complexity when explaining your solutions. 
• 
Be confident in your projects and clearly explain the technologies used, design choices, and 
outcomes. 
• 
Communicate your answers in a structured and confident manner. 
• 
Prepare for behavioral questions such as teamwork, conflict management, strengths, and 
weaknesses. 
• 
Stay calm during the interview and admit honestly if you don’t know something, while trying 
to reason it out. 
• 
Show genuine interest in the company by asking thoughtful questions at the end of the 
interview.

Coding Question – 1: 
It was based on sorting.  
Given an array, e.g., [12, 13, 6], sort it in ascending order according to the number of factors of 
each element in the array. The output of the question is [13,6,12].  
Explanation:  
12 → Factors: 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 12 → 6 factors 
13 → Factors: 1, 13 → 2 factors 
6 → Factors: 1, 2, 3, 6 → 4 factors 
Sorting them by the number of factors (ascending order): 
13 → 2 factors 
6 → 4 factors 
12 → 6 factors 
Hence, the output is [13, 6, 12]. 
Actually this was an very very simple question, our only task was to write optimal algorithm for 
finding the No. of factors of an element. I wrote that algorithm that finds the factors in O(root 
N) time where N is the  element for which factors are need to be found.  
After writing a function for finding the No. of factors, then I used In- build Sort function in C++, 
and just passes an input array, and an Custom Comparator where the element are compared 
according to the factors. I used that factors function in this custom comparator. 
Hence, within five minutes I was able to solve this problem and all the 15 test cases passed.  
Coding Question-2:  
I don’t exactly remember the question now, It was like N organic substances will be given, 
each organic substance will have two values like growth factor and additive factor. Our goal 
was to find the minimum absolute difference Between growth factor and additive factor 
across all possible combinations of organic substances.  How the growth factor and additive 
factor was calculated for combination of organic substances is the multiplication of all the 
growth factors in that combination is the new growth factor for that whole combination and 
similarly the addition of the  additive factors in that combination is the new additive factor for 
that combination. 
 
I solved that problem using Recursion first, like just finding all the possible subsequences  
While finding itself will multiply the growth factor and add additive factor and then finding the 
minimum once end index is reached. But that solution was not accepted for some test cases. 
Then later I solved this using power set algorithm which is used for finding subsequences. 
Both take same time c but space c differs since  power set doesn’t use recursion stack space 
just uses bit manipulation and loops.  
Then for this problem also all the 15 test cases was passed after using that power set algo. 
Actually I didn’t read this question, I directly went to sample input and output and I was able 
to understand what we have to do and find.  
Anyone who solved subset sum problem can solve this problem to.  
Coding Question – 3:

E 1 2

Interview Round- 1 : 
This is my first interview, and it was held at CUIC, and I was also very nervous. But the 
interviewer spoke well and was good. 
 
First, he asked me to give a brief introduction about myself. Then he started with a basic 
aptitude math question, like when two dice that have 6 faces are rolled, what is the probability 
of the sum of the faces being prime? I wrote the sample space on the paper, I was explaining 
the answer, and he was satisfied with that answer. Then the follow-up question was, if we roll

Accolite  
 
It consists of 5 rounds. 
Round 1: Aptitude round 
 
There were aptitude questions based on Operating Systems, Computer 
Networks, Database Management Systems, Quantitative Aptitude, Logical reasoning. A 
mix up of all these aptitudes rounding to 30 questions. This test was held on EduThrill 
platform and the total time given was around 30-40 minutes. You really need to be very 
quick in solving those quantitative aptitude which will boost your time to analyze the 
technical aptitude questions. Most of the students except a few were selected for the 
next coding round. You need to answer a reasonably correct number of questions to get 
selected to the next round. 
 
 
Round 2: Coding round 
 
In this round, 2 coding questions were asked and both of them were very easy 
ones. With a little bit of practice one could solve them both in 5-10 minutes. I would 
highly recommend to practice coding in Hackerrank platform for beginners and Geeks 
for geeks and Code-chef platform for intermediates and advanced level coders. Try 
completing the must do interview questions available in Geeks for geeks well ahead of 
the placement season. The below are the questions asked 
1. Trapping rain water  
(https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/trapping-rain-water/) 
2. Happy arrays 
(https://codechef.com/problems/HAPPYARR (Not a same question but similar to this)) 
 
Mainly the Coding round was fully focused on solving real time problems and it 
was held on Codelyzer platform. Candidates can use any language of their choice for 
solving the coding questions. Since the questions were easy many were able to solve 
them both, hence the selection criteria were based on space and time complexity and 
also the time taken by the candidate to solve the problems. The candidates selected for 
the face to face interviews were announced 2 hours post the coding round. Around 80 
were shortlisted for the next round. All the face to face interviews were held over skype. 
 
 
Round 3: Technical interview 
The interview began with formal greetings. She then asked me to introduce myself and 
had a casual talk with me to make me feel comfortable. The following questions were 
asked 
 
1. The task is to divide an array into K non-empty subsets such that the sum of 
elements in every subset is same. All elements of this array should be part of 
exactly one partition. 
 
2. The task is to find if there is a triplet in an array whose sum is equal to the given 
value. 
 
3. The task is to split the given array into K subarrays (they must cover all the 
elements). The maximum subarray sum achievable out of K subarrays formed, 
must be minimum possible. Find that possible subarray sum. 
 
4. She then tested my knowledge on binary trees by asking about the different kind 
of traversals like inorder, postorder, preorder, level order and asked me the 
difference between then with an example. 
 
The below are some links which will help you to solve those questions. 
https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/partition-set-k-subsets-equal-sum/ 
https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/split-the-given-array-into-k-sub-arrays-such-that-
maximum-sum-of-all-sub-arrays-is-minimum/ 
https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/find-a-triplet-that-sum-to-a-given-value/ 
https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/tree-traversals-inorder-preorder-and-postorder/ 
 
WHAT I SUGGEST TO YOU FROM THIS ROUND : 
 Give a strong introduction about yourself since that is the first impression about 
you to the interviewer. 
 Take some time to analyze the problem. Break the entire problems into sub 
problems and try solving the sub problems to arrive at your answers. 
 Discuss your approach with your interviewer and make them involved in your 
approach. 
 Try cracking those corner cases first to impress your interviewer. 
 
Many were unable to crack this round due to its level of hardness but luckily I was on 
the other side. The next interview was held after 15 minutes post completion of this 
round. 
 
Round 4: Technical interview 
 
The interviewer asked me to introduce myself and I was asked questions on my 
resume. We had a long chat on my summer research intern project and then the 
interview began. 
1. Given a Binary Tree, convert it to a Doubly Linked List. The left and right pointers 
in nodes are to be used as previous and next pointers respectively in converted 
DLL. The order of nodes in DLL must be same as inorder of the given Binary 
Tree. The first node of inorder traversal (left most node in BT) must be head 
node of the DLL. 
 
Note: To be very honest, I became nervous on seeing this question but I 
didn’t give up and started to suggest some approaches. The interviewer 
then found that I was not so comfortable with this question and gave me 
another question. 
2. Find Maximum Length of a sub-string without any repetitions. 
- 
For “ABDEFGABEF”, the longest substring are “BDEFGA” and 
“DEFGAB”, with length 6. 
- 
For “BBBB” the longest substring is “B”, with length 1. 
 
3. Given a string s1 and a string s2, write a snippet to say whether s2 is a rotation 
of s1? 
(eg given s1 = ABCD and s2 = CDAB, return true, given s1 = ABCD, and s2 = 
ACBD , return false) 
 
4. Reverse a linked list in groups of given sizes 
5. A puzzle to test my problem solving capability   
https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/puzzle-1000-light-bulbs-switched-on-off-by-1000-
persons-passing-by/ (not the exact one but with some modification in the 
numbers)  
 
WHAT I SUGGEST TO YOU FROM THIS ROUND : 
 Be honest with the interviewer if you are asked whether you find it difficult or not , 
be honest with him but suggest some approaches to express your “never give 
up” attitude. 
 Always try to optimize your solutions if you are asked to. Have good knowledge 
of the time and space complexities. 
 Practice a lot of puzzles and riddles as solving them show off your problem 
solving skills.  
 Always discuss your approach with the interviewer as he/she may give some 
hints to optimize your approach, never keep your interviewer idle. 
At the end of this round, the interviewer was very much impressed with my performance 
and he connected the call to his manager immediately for my HR round. 
  
Round 5: HR interview 
 
The manager asked me to introduce myself and started testing my 
communication and thinking out-the-box skills. He asked me about the Covid-19 impact 
on Tamil Nadu and questioned me on the effectiveness of the measures taken by the 
Government during this pandemic and also asked me to suggest some measures if I 
were given the authority. Then we had a chat about Indian railways giving free Wifi 
access to its passengers (good/bad/what else could be done), climate in my area of 
residence and so on. 
 
Later he questioned me on aptitudes regarding Distance/Speed and 
Time/Clocks. He even gave me two problems to solve on finding the angle between 
minute and hour hand. Later he was very satisfied and appreciated me for my 
performance. 
Finally 11 of them were selected and I got my name on that list. :) 
 
 
WHAT I SUGGEST TO YOU FROM THIS ROUND : 
 Be prepared with quantitative aptitudes as it can even help you in your HR round 
as it did in my case. I would suggest websites like Indiabix, Freshers world etc. 
 
 Even though I was not asked any formal HR questions like 1. Why should I hire 
you? 2.What are your strengths and weakness , be prepared to answer them as 
well as banking and investment sectors which are on for placements would ask 
them for sure. 
 
Topics to be focused: 
1. Data structures & Algorithms. 
2. Competitive coding/Problem solving. 
3. Object Oriented Programming Language for implementation in the interview. 
(C++/JAVA is mostly preferred by the interviewers) 
4. Projects mentioned in your resume. 
5. Aptitude (Verbal/Reasoning/Quantitative) 
6. Communication (Be prepared with your answers for famous HR questions) 
 
ALL THE BEST!!!

Citi – Intern Interview Experience 
 
 
About the company 
Citi is a global bank that offers a variety of financial services, including 
banking, credit cards, and investment solutions. It operates in more than 160 
countries and values innovation, diversity, and teamwork, creating an inclusive 
workplace where employees can grow. Citi’s mission is to help people, businesses, 
and communities achieve their financial goals. 
Role: Application Developer (App Dev) 
Stipend: 75,000 INR per month 
Duration of internship: 8-10 weeks 
 
 
Selection Process : 
The Selection process had 3 rounds. 
1. Online test 
2. Technical Interview 
3. HR Interview 
 
1. Online test (12.10.25) 
This online test had coding basically. 
1st question - Temperature_Check 
2nd question - Shortest_Unique_Substring 
3rd question - Largest_Square 
 
The first question was very simple. We can solve it just by using a switch 
statement. The problem was like the user gives a temperature value , we have to 
determine the range of it and return the comments given as per the question. 
 
The second question was simple. It was similar to the problem 
Shortest_Unique_Substring. 
 
 
The third question was like creating  a algorithm for the given question 
which was in medium difficulty . I could not remember the question exactly . 
 
 
As the test was in online, completion time was one of the factor for getting 
shortlisted for the interview .
 
 
 
2. Technical Interview (05.11.2025) 
A total of 82 students from CEG and MIT was shortlisted for technical 
interview. Both HR and Technical interviews were in-person interviews which held 
at CUIC, CEG. 
The interviews were conducted in the Auditorium of CUIC, with students being 
randomly assigned to each panel. All panels had a two interviewers. Randomly, I was 
assigned to the most difficult panel. 
After greeting the interviewers, I handed them my resume. The interview 
began with the interviewers introducing themselves, followed by them asking me to 
introduce myself. 
Be prepared with the self-introduction as it makes the first impression. I 
started off with the self-intro as instructed. As I had mentioned about my extra-
curricular activites explicitly. We had a conversation about my cricketing journey for 
2-3 minutes. 
After reviewing my resume, they told me that they weren’t interested about 
the projects that I had put on my resume as all the people who attended the 
interview was having similar kind of projects using MERN stack, DBMS etc…. So, they 
told me that they are moving to the fundamentals of CS(OS, OOPS, DBMS, Networks 
and  Data Communication), and about programming languages  mainly JAVA. 
He first started asking questions about JAVA 
• 
What is JVM? 
• 
Difference between JVM and JRE. 
• 
How does the complier compile the .java file ? and he asked me to explain the 
flow of control. 
• 
What compiler is used in JAVA? 
Then he moved onto DBMS where the questions were 
• 
He gave me a description about the table schema and I had to create a table 
for the description which he gave and write it down on a paper. After writing 
he checked it. 
• 
He asked me to create an index for a column in that table and asked me to 
write it down. 
Then the interviewer moved onto OS 
• 
He asked me about the concepts of OS and I explained him about the concept. 
• 
He asked me whether I am familiar with Linux OS. 
• 
He asked me to explain some Linux commands using an example with its 
usage 
 
 
Then he moved onto OOPS 
• 
He asked me about Polymorphism . 
• 
I was asked to write the code for Static polymorphism and he checked it. 
• 
Then he asked me to explain Run-time polymorphism. 
• 
In inheritance he asked me a question stating that “Class A has a numerous 
methods. Class B is inherited from Class A. Now I have to access the class A’s 
method using the object of class B. Is it possible?”. And he asked me to write 
the code of that particular line. 
Then he moved onto Full Stack 
• 
What is frontend and backend? 
• 
How to frontend and backend communicate with each other? And I was asked 
to write the code on the paper and explain him. 
• 
He gave me a situation that, “The API from frontend transmit the data to the 
backend and the API that should catch it is down. What would happen?”. I 
replied him and the asked “What kind of error type and number ?“. I answered 
the question and he asked “Why not 404?”. I explained him and he gave many 
code asked me find out where it is used. 
The second interviewer was listening to me till then and he asked a 
question about the working of MySQL in the backend like how does the query  
“Select  * from “ takes the records from the table and what data structure is 
during the retrieval process of the query. He asked me to try and explain it. I told 
my approach using trees(B trees) and he asked me “why not graph ?”. So I 
changed my approach using the Graph using BFS and DFS. Then I was asked to 
explain about the BFS process. 
Finally, both the interviewers were satisfied my answers and they gave me 
a chance to ask them questions. 
The first round (Technical round) got over. It was around 1 hr. 
Everything depends upon the interviewer. Some interviewers asks less 
questions. While some of the interviewers go in depth about your project and the 
fundamentals. So be prepared for both. 
 
2. HR Interview (05.10.2024) 
From the 82 students who were qualified for technical round, 30 were 
selected for the next round (HR). 
It was a one-to-one interview. Since she had a copy of my resume on her 
hand, I didn’t hand over my resume. She began with her introduction, and then I 
followed with my own introduction. When I mentioned my extra-curricular 
activities, she was impressed with it and I also added my 12th mark which 
impressed her more. 
I was asked questions such as “Where do you see yourself in 5 years? “,then 
she enquired about my parents details, my CGPA, any current backlogs, some family 
details and whether I had any idea of higher studies. 
She asked me if I had any questions. With this the HR round of interview 
came to an end. This round lasted for about 10 minutes. 
Out of 30 people, 12 were selected for App dev intern role, and by god’s grace 
I was one among them. 
 
 
All the best!

Verizon Interview Experience 
Verizon is a leading global communications and technology company, ranked as the #1 
wireless provider in the United States and a global leader in next-generation technologies 
such as 5G and the Internet of Things (IoT). Verizon is recognized for its innovation, 
advanced network technology, and enterprise solutions. In 2024, Verizon was ranked as the 
8th Best Workplace Worldwide by LinkedIn, highlighting its strong culture, employee 
development, and leadership in technology. Working at Verizon provides opportunities for 
professional growth, exposure to cutting-edge tools, and being part of a globally respected 
brand. 
Recruitment Process 
1. Online Assessment (Aon Cocubes) 
The online assessment evaluated multiple skills: 
• 
Coding Round (2 Questions): 
1. Check if the sum of digits of a number N is prime  
2. Given N, T, energy gains , and jumps  calculate the minimum hours required 
to reach or exceed target energy. 
• 
Essay Writing: Tested critical thinking and writing skills. 
• 
Language Proficiency: A 4-minute rapid-fire section to answer as many grammar, 
spelling, and vocabulary questions as possible. Wrong answers had negative marks, 
but skipped questions carried no penalty—speed and accuracy were key. 
• 
Puzzle Section: Medium-level puzzles in a rapid-fire format tested logical reasoning 
and problem-solving speed. 
Outcome: 82 candidates were shortlisted for the GD round. 
2. Group Discussion (GD) 
• 
Topic: How India Serves as a Consumer Base for Global Market Industries 
• 
Format: 9 participants per panel, 20 minutes. 
Tips: 
• 
Focus on quality points, not quantity. 
• 
Don’t dominate but participate meaningfully. 
• 
Use examples or data to support your ideas. 
• 
Acknowledge and build on others’ points politely. 
• 
Maintain confident body language and show active listening. 
 Outcome: 50 candidates were shortlisted for the next round. 
3. AI Prompting Round 
This round was not preliminary but a key part of the process. Candidates solved problem 
statements (Realtime System failure concepts, Marketing Solution)using Prompt Engineering 
techniques such as one-shot prompting, role-based prompting, and structured prompts. 
Tips: 
• 
Understand the problem clearly before writing your prompt. 
• 
Use concise instructions with context. 
• 
Experiment with role-based prompts for better results. 
• 
Practice different prompting strategies in advance. 
• 
Stay updated on advancements in AI tools and methods. 
 Outcome: No eliminations took place in this round. 
4. Technical Interview 
My technical interview lasted 45 minutes, but durations can vary. If the panel finds you 
reliable and knowledgeable, they may extend the questioning. 
Key Areas Covered: 
• 
Programming Languages: Be thorough in the languages you mention. 
• 
Java Concepts: Core Java, OOP principles, and sometimes interface-related coding 
tasks. 
• 
Generative AI and Prompt Engineering: Questions on GenAI models, use cases, and 
prompt strategies. 
• 
Projects: Detailed discussions on project schemas, architecture, challenges, and 
contributions. 
• 
Panel Variation: Questions may vary depending on the panel. 
Tips: 
• 
Know your resume inside-out—anything you list can be asked. 
• 
Be well-prepared for programming fundamentals and AI advancements. 
• 
Understand your project schema and architecture in detail. 
• 
Revise Java basics, data structures, DBMS, and OS. 
• 
Be honest if unsure—explain your thought process. 
• 
Connect your experiences to Verizon’s technologies and values. 
 Outcome: 32 candidates advanced to the HR round, and all were finally selected. 
5. HR Round 
The HR round was casual and friendly, focusing on cultural fit. 
Questions Asked: Family background, hometown, interests, and flexibility for relocation. 
Tips: 
• 
Be genuine and confident. 
• 
Show enthusiasm for Verizon’s culture and opportunities. 
• 
Prepare thoughtful questions about growth and company culture. 
Outcome: Confirmation mails and shortlisted names were shared after HR—not immediate 
offer letters. 
 
Additional Tips for Success 
• 
Coding: Practice competitive programming, focus on time complexity and edge 
cases. 
• 
GD: Stay informed on current affairs and global market trends, especially India’s role 
in global trade. 
• 
AI Prompting: Learn few-shot, zero-shot, and role-based prompting. 
• 
Technical: Be thorough with programming languages, project schema, and AI tool 
advancements. Expect extended questioning if you’re deemed reliable. 
• 
General Advice: Stay calm, answer confidently, and maintain a positive attitude—
confidence matters as much as correctness. 
Summary 
Verizon’s recruitment process was smooth and transparent, testing coding skills, 
communication, 
AI 
prompting 
techniques, 
and 
technical 
expertise.

ROYAL BANK OF SCOTLAND (RBS) 
 
 The RBS Group is a large international banking and financial services company. The Group 
provides a comprehensive range of financial services to retail, corporate, and financial 
institutions. 
Placement Process: 
 
The applicants were filtered based on CGPA and then the placement procedure took place in 
two rounds. The first online test round and an online face-to-face interview round. 
 
Round 1: Aptitude, Verbal, Technical Round: 
First round was for 2 hours on Mettl tests platform. It consisted of MCQs and 3 coding 
questions. 
MCQ Questions were based on Quantitative aptitude (profit loss, probability etc.), Logical 
Reasoning (directions, blood relations etc.), Verbal Aptitude (language related questions like 
synonyms, antonyms and comprehension type) and Technical Questions (mostly related to 
DBMS, OS and data structures). The coding questions were array-based.  
After completing the first round, the toppers were selected for the second interview round. 
 
Round 2: Technical and HR Round: 
This round consists of 2 panels (Technical and HR) 
Technical Questions: 
1. Introduce yourself. 
2. What are the subjects you are familiar with and which are you most comfortable? 
I chose Database Management Systems, so most of the questions were based on 
DBMS. 
3. What is Normalization? 
4. What is 2NF and explain with an example used in your project. 
5. What is a trigger and have you used it in your project? What are the types of triggers? 
6. What is a stored procedure? What are the advantages/disadvantages of using a stored 
procedure rather than executing as individual queries separately? 
7. What are some methods to improve the speed of execution of database queries? 
(Indexing etc.) 
 
 
 
Project Related questions: 
 
I had mentioned 3 projects in my resume and they chose two and asked to explain 
them in detail. 
The asked the following questions about the android app I had developed. 
1. What version of Android is your app compatible with? Why did you choose that 
version?  
2. What language is it developed in and what tools have you used? 
3. Did you have any way for clearing up the data to free up space once the data is not 
needed? (housekeeping) 
4. What is the difference between your application and the already existing ones? 
5. Have you released the application on the play store?  
HR Questions: 
1. What is good teamwork according to you? What are the characteristics of a good 
team? 
2. What is your aim? 
3. What do you know about RBS from PPT and what do you think you'll be working on? 
4. How do you keep yourself updated about technology? 
5. If people in your team listen to your idea, but do not implement it, how will you 
react? 
6. What is your favourite domain? What will you do if you get a project in a domain that 
you are not interested in? 
These were the questions asked to me. I had not answered 1-2 questions with clarity but 
overall it was a good experience.  
Topics to be focussed: 
 Database Management Systems 
 Data Structures and Algorithms 
 Operating Systems 
 
Takeaway: 
Having a good knowledge of the basics of DBMS, OS and Data structures, having clear idea 
about everything mentioned in the resume and confidence and clarity to answer the HR 
questions will help to get placed in RBS. 
Thank you. All the Best.
